t]][[Category:completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:ted]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun}} {{Japanese Kofun |Name = {{ill|Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}} |image = |Alternate names = |Group = |Location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}{{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市}} Shibazaki Town (柴崎町) |coordinatess = 36 |coordinatess = 18 |coordinatess = 52.58 |coordinatess = 139 |coordinatess = 3 |coordinatess = 24.43 |ISO = JP-10 |Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} or {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳}} |map_size = Diameter 12 m or side length 22 m |Burial facility = (Estimated) {{ill|Clay Coffin|ja|粘土槨}} |Excavated items = 2 {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}s (including 1 bronze mirror with an inscription from the first year of the Seishi era) and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡}}s, as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |Secondary tumulus = |Period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |Interred = |Mausoleum = |Historic Site = None |Tangible Cultural Property = Excavated items (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}) |Notes = The mound is no longer extant |map = Japan Gunma |Icon = |map_dot_label = Shibazaki
Kani-sawa Kofun |map_dot_label position = top }} '''Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun''' (柴崎蟹沢古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shibazaki Town, {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. Its shape was either a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} or a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳}}. The mound is no longer extant, and the excavated items have been designated as a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Overview == It is a small kofun constructed on a low terrace on the southern bank of the Kasu River (粕川), a branch of the Ino River, in the southern part of Gunma Prefecture. In the surrounding area, the distribution of Early Kofun period sites and kofun is known, and to the southeast lies the {{ill|Shibazaki Asama-yama Kofun|ja|柴崎浅間山古墳}}. In {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), grave goods were excavated, and subsequently the mound was leveled. The original shape of the tumulus is unclear; {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾}} regarded it as a small {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} of about 12 meters in diameter, while {{ill|Motomura Gōshō|ja|本村豪章}} considered it a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳}} with a side length of about 22 meters{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. No thatched stones or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were observed on the mound’s exterior{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. According to records, the burial facility is estimated to have been a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}; its main axis was oriented north–south, and traces of vermilion were found inside{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. During the excavation in {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), four bronze mirrors (2 {{ill|triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors|ja|内行花文鏡}}) as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} were recovered{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Notably, one of the triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors bears an inscription indicating the first year of the era "□始元年" (presumably the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始}} of the Wei, i.e. {{ill|340|ja|340}}), making it an important example of the early stage in the development of such mirrors{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The construction period is estimated to be in the latter half of the 4th century of the Early Kofun period{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Despite its small size, this kofun is significant because, in addition to yielding one of the few dated mirrors in Gunma Prefecture, it also contained four bronze mirrors as grave goods. These features—unreflected in the tumulus’s shape or map_size—suggest a connection with the Yamato state in the Kinai region, similar only to the {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳}} and the {{ill|Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|ja|長者屋敷天王山古墳}} in Gunma Prefecture{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The excavated items were designated as a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in {{ill|1989|ja|1989}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}}). == Site History == * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), grave goods were excavated{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * In {{ill|1926|ja|1926}} (the first year of the {{ill|Showa era|ja|昭和}}), {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾}} conducted a survey (at which time the mound had been leveled and converted to farmland){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * On June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}}), the excavated items were designated as a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990}} (Heisei 2), a survey was conducted on residential land in the Shimo-Ōruimachi area, but the kofun could not be confirmed (as recorded in the Takasaki City History){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. == Excavated Items == [[File:蟹沢古墳出土 三角縁同向式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Triangular-edged, same-facing Shikishinju Mirror
(National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}})}}{{small|{{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} exhibition.}}]] The grave goods excavated in {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} are as follows{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}: * Bronze mirrors: 4 ** {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}: 2 ***: One mirror is a triangular-edged, same-facing (stair-step type) Shikishinju mirror, with a diameter of 22.6 cm and a rim thickness of 1.0 cm. It bears the inscription "□始元年 陳是作鏡 自有經迷 本自𦭴師 杜地命出 壽如金石 保子□□" and is presumed to date to the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始}} ({{ill|340|ja|340}}) of the Wei, representing an early stage of triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors. Mirrors of similar type have been found at the {{ill|Moriho Kofun|ja|森尾古墳}} in Hyogo Prefecture and the {{ill|Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|ja|竹島御家老屋敷古墳}} in Yamaguchi Prefecture. ***: Another mirror is a triangular-edged mirror depicting three gods and three beasts, with a diameter of 21.9 cm and a rim thickness of 2.1 cm. Its condition is notably poor. Similar mirrors have been found at the {{ill|Niiyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture, the {{ill|Manai Kofun|ja|真名井古墳}} in Osaka Prefecture, and a site in southern Kyoto (now in the collection of the Kurashiki Archaeological Museum). ** {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡}}: 2 ***: One mirror is an eight-flower (Hakka) mirror, with a diameter of 10.14 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. ***: Another mirror is a six-flower (Rokka) mirror, with a diameter of 9.8 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. * Iron axes: 2 *: Rectangular iron axes. One piece measures 12.4 cm in length, 4.3 cm in width, and 1.0 cm in thickness; the other measures 8.4 cm in length, 4.4 cm in width, and 1.3 cm in thickness. * Iron adze: 1 *: Some sources refer to it as an iron spear. It is double-edged, with a remaining length of 18.1 cm and a blade width of 1.0 cm. * Fragments of iron swords: 4 fragmentary pieces. * Fragments of a straight sword: with a remaining length of 22.3 cm. * Fragments of {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}: 9 pieces, representing three types – a double-mouthed jar, an S-shaped jar with a rim base, and a jar with high shoulders. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Gunma Prefecture Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun Excavated Items (archaeological materials) – details are as follows. These items are held by the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} and were designated on June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}}){{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳出土品}}. ** Bronze mirrors: *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror with an inscription indicating the first year of □始元年. *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror. *** 2 Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors. ** Swords and related items: *** Fragments of iron swords – collectively. *** Fragments of iron sword remnants – collectively. ** 2 pieces of fragmented iron axes. ** 1 piece of fragmented iron halberd. ** 9 pieces of fragmented {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (located in Taitō, Tokyo) – holds the excavated items from the Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Saburō Kobayashi (archaeologist)|ja|小林三郎 (考古学者)|lt=Saburō Kobayashi|lt_ja=小林三郎}}|year=1989|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|lt=Nihon Kofun Daijiten}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|蟹沢古墳（古墳）|1989}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=|year=1999|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title=New Edited Takasaki City History|volume=Materials Edition 1: Early Ancient I|publisher={{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Bronze mirror#Inscriptions|ja|銅鏡#紀年銘鏡}} == External Links == * {{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳出土品}} {{citation needed|しはさきかにさわこふん}} [[Category:Ancient Tombs in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takasaki City]] [[Category:National Important Cultural Properties of Tokyo]] [[Category:translated pages]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category: tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:柴崎蟹沢古墳]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}})}}{{small|{{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} exhibition.}}]] The grave goods excavated in {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} are as follows{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}: * Bronze mirrors: 4 ** {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}: 2 ***: One mirror is a triangular-edged, same-facing (stair-step type) Shikishinju mirror, with a diameter of 22.6 cm and a rim thickness of 1.0 cm. These items are held by the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} and were designated on June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}}){{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳出土品}}. ** Bronze mirrors: *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror with an inscription indicating the first year of □始元年. *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror. *** 2 Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors. ** Swords and related items: *** Fragments of iron swords – collectively. *** Fragments of iron sword remnants – collectively. ** 2 pieces of fragmented iron axes. ** 1 piece of fragmented iron halberd. ** 9 pieces of fragmented {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (located Kanizawa Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun}} {{Japanese Kofun |Name = {{ill|Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}} |image = |Alternate names = |Group = |Location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}{{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市}} Shibazaki Town (柴崎町) |coordinatess = 36 |coordinatess = 18 |coordinatess = 52.58 |coordinatess = 139 |coordinatess = 3 |coordinatess = 24.43 |ISO = JP-10 |Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} or {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳}} |map_size = Diameter 12 m or side length 22 m |Burial Facility = (Estimated) {{ill|Clay Coffin|ja|粘土槨}} |excavations = 2 {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}s (including 1 bronze mirror with an inscription from the first year of the Seishi era) and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡}}s, as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |Secondary tumulus = |Period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |Interred = |Mausoleum = |Historic Site = None |Tangible Cultural Property = excavations (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}) |Notes = The mound is no longer extant |map = Japan Gunma |Icon = |map_dot_label = Shibazaki
Kani-sawa Kofun |map_dot_label position = top }} '''Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun''' (柴崎蟹沢古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shibazaki Town, {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. Its shape was either a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} or a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳}}. The mound is no longer extant, and the excavated items have been designated as a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Overview == It is a small kofun constructed on a low terrace on the southern bank of the Kasu River (粕川), a branch of the Ino River, in the southern part of Gunma Prefecture. In the surrounding area, the distribution of Early Kofun period sites and kofun is known, and to the southeast lies the {{ill|Shibazaki Asama-yama Kofun|ja|柴崎浅間山古墳}}. In {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), grave goods were excavated, and subsequently the mound was leveled. The original shape of the tumulus is unclear; {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾}} regarded it as a small {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} of about 12 meters in diameter, while {{ill|Motomura Gōshō|ja|本村豪章}} considered it a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳}} with a side length of about 22 meters{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. No thatched stones or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were observed on the mound’s exterior{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. According to records, the burial facility is estimated to have been a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}; its main axis was oriented north–south, and traces of vermilion were found inside{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. During the excavation in {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), four bronze mirrors (2 {{ill|triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors|ja|内行花文鏡}}) as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} were recovered{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Notably, one of the triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors bears an inscription indicating the first year of the era "□始元年" (presumably the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始}} of the Wei, i.e. {{ill|340|ja|340}}), making it an important example of the early stage in the development of such mirrors{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The construction period is estimated to be in the latter half of the 4th century of the Early Kofun period{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Despite its small size, this kofun is significant because, in addition to yielding one of the few dated mirrors in Gunma Prefecture, it also contained four bronze mirrors as grave goods. These features—unreflected in the tumulus’s shape or map_size—suggest a connection with the Yamato state in the Kinai region, similar only to the {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳}} and the {{ill|Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|ja|長者屋敷天王山古墳}} in Gunma Prefecture{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The excavated items were designated as a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in {{ill|1989|ja|1989}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}}). == Site History == * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), grave goods were excavated{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * In {{ill|1926|ja|1926}} (the first year of the {{ill|Showa era|ja|昭和}}), {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾}} conducted a survey (at which time the mound had been leveled and converted to farmland){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * On June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}}), the excavated items were designated as a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990}} (Heisei 2), a survey was conducted on residential land in the Shimo-Ōruimachi area, but the kofun could not be confirmed (as recorded in the Takasaki City History){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. == Excavated Items == [[File:蟹沢古墳出土 三角縁同向式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Triangular-edged, same-facing Shikishinju Mirror
(National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}})}}{{small|{{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} exhibition.}}]] The grave goods excavated in {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} are as follows{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}: * Bronze mirrors: 4 ** {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}: 2 ***: One mirror is a triangular-edged, same-facing (stair-step type) Shikishinju mirror, with a diameter of 22.6 cm and a rim thickness of 1.0 cm. It bears the inscription "□始元年 陳是作鏡 自有經迷 本自𦭴師 杜地命出 壽如金石 保子□□" and is presumed to date to the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始}} ({{ill|340|ja|340}}) of the Wei, representing an early stage of triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors. Mirrors of similar type have been found at the {{ill|Moriho Kofun|ja|森尾古墳}} in Hyogo Prefecture and the {{ill|Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|ja|竹島御家老屋敷古墳}} in Yamaguchi Prefecture. ***: Another mirror is a triangular-edged mirror depicting three gods and three beasts, with a diameter of 21.9 cm and a rim thickness of 2.1 cm. Its condition is notably poor. Similar mirrors have been found at the {{ill|Niiyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture, the {{ill|Manai Kofun|ja|真名井古墳}} in Osaka Prefecture, and a site in southern Kyoto (now in the collection of the Kurashiki Archaeological Museum). ** {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡}}: 2 ***: One mirror is an eight-flower (Hakka) mirror, with a diameter of 10.14 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. ***: Another mirror is a six-flower (Rokka) mirror, with a diameter of 9.8 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. * Iron axes: 2 *: Rectangular iron axes. One piece measures 12.4 cm in length, 4.3 cm in width, and 1.0 cm in thickness; the other measures 8.4 cm in length, 4.4 cm in width, and 1.3 cm in thickness. * Iron adze: 1 *: Some sources refer to it as an iron spear. It is double-edged, with a remaining length of 18.1 cm and a blade width of 1.0 cm. * Fragments of iron swords: 4 fragmentary pieces. * Fragments of a straight sword: with a remaining length of 22.3 cm. * Fragments of {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}: 9 pieces, representing three types – a double-mouthed jar, an S-shaped jar with a rim base, and a jar with high shoulders. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Gunma Prefecture Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun Excavated Items (archaeological materials) – details are as follows. These items are held by the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} and were designated on June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}}){{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳出土品}}. ** Bronze mirrors: *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror with an inscription indicating the first year of □始元年. *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror. *** 2 Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors. ** Swords and related items: *** Fragments of iron swords – collectively. *** Fragments of iron sword remnants – collectively. ** 2 pieces of fragmented iron axes. ** 1 piece of fragmented iron halberd. ** 9 pieces of fragmented {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (located in Taitō, Tokyo) – holds the excavated items from the Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Saburō Kobayashi (archaeologist)|ja|小林三郎 (考古学者)|lt=Saburō Kobayashi|lt_ja=小林三郎}}|year=1989|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|lt=Nihon Kofun Daijiten}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|蟹沢古墳（古墳）|1989}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=|year=1999|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title=New Edited Takasaki City History|volume=Materials Edition 1: Early Ancient I|publisher={{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Bronze mirror#Inscriptions|ja|銅鏡#紀年銘鏡}} == External Links == * {{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳出土品}} {{citation needed|しはさきかにさわこふん}} [[Category:Ancient Tombs in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takasaki City]] [[Category:National Despite its small size, this kofun is significant because, in addition to yielding one of the few dated mirrors in Gunma Prefecture, it also contained four bronze mirrors as grave goods. These features—unreflected in the tumulus’s shape or map_size—suggest a connection with the Yamato state in the Kinai region, similar only to the {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳}} and the {{ill|Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|ja|長者屋敷天王山古墳}} in Gunma Prefecture{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The excavated items were designated as a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in {{ill|1989|ja|1989}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}}). == Site History == * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), grave goods were excavated{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * In {{ill|1926|ja|1926}} (the first year of the {{ill|Showa era|ja|昭和}}), {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾}} conducted a survey (at which time the mound had been leveled and converted to farmland){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * On June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}}), the excavated items were designated as a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990}} (Heisei 2), a survey was conducted on residential land in the Shimo-Ōruimachi area, but the kofun could not be confirmed (as recorded in the Takasaki City History){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. == Excavated Items == [[File:蟹沢古墳出土 三角縁同向式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Triangular-edged, same-facing Shikishinju Mirror
(National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}})}}{{small|{{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} exhibition.}}]] The grave goods excavated in {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} are as follows{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}: * Bronze mirrors: 4 ** {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}: 2 ***: One mirror is a triangular-edged, same-facing (stair-step type) Shikishinju mirror, with a diameter of 22.6 cm and a rim thickness of 1.0 cm. It bears the inscription "□始元年 陳是作鏡 自有經迷 本自𦭴師 杜地命出 壽如金石 保子□□" and is presumed to date to the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始}} ({{ill|340|ja|340}}) of the Wei, representing an early stage of triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors. Mirrors of similar type have been found at the {{ill|Moriho Kofun|ja|森尾古墳}} in Hyogo Prefecture and the {{ill|Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|ja|竹島御家老屋敷古墳}} in Yamaguchi Prefecture. ***: Another mirror is a triangular-edged mirror depicting three gods and three beasts, with a diameter of 21.9 cm and a rim thickness of 2.1 cm. Its condition is notably poor. Similar mirrors have been found at the {{ill|Niiyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture, the {{ill|Manai Kofun|ja|真名井古墳}} in Osaka Prefecture, and a site in southern Kyoto (now in the collection of the Kurashiki Archaeological Museum). ** {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡}}: 2 ***: One mirror is an eight-flower (Hakka) mirror, with a diameter of 10.14 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. ***: Another mirror is a six-flower (Rokka) mirror, with a diameter of 9.8 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. * Iron axes: 2 *: Rectangular iron axes. One piece measures 12.4 cm in length, 4.3 cm in width, and 1.0 cm in thickness; the other measures 8.4 cm in length, 4.4 cm in width, and 1.3 cm in thickness. * Iron adze: 1 *: Some sources refer to it as an iron spear. It is double-edged, with a remaining length of 18.1 cm and a blade width of 1.0 cm. * Fragments of iron swords: 4 fragmentary pieces. * Fragments of a straight sword: with a remaining length of 22.3 cm. * Fragments of {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}: 9 pieces, representing three types – a double-mouthed jar, an S-shaped jar with a rim base, and a jar with high shoulders. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Gunma Prefecture Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun Excavated Items (archaeological materials) – details are as follows. These items are held by the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} and were designated on June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}}){{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳出土品}}. ** Bronze mirrors: *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror with an inscription indicating the first year of □始元年. *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror. *** 2 Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors. ** Swords and related items: *** Fragments of iron swords – collectively. *** Fragments of iron sword remnants – collectively. ** 2 pieces of fragmented iron axes. ** 1 piece of fragmented iron halberd. ** 9 pieces of fragmented {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (located in Taitō, Tokyo) – holds the excavated items from the Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Saburō Kobayashi Kani-sawa Kofun |map_dot_label position = top }} '''Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun''' (柴崎蟹沢古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shibazaki Town, {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. Its shape was either a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} or a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳}}. The mound is no longer extant, and the excavated items have been designated as a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Overview == It is a small kofun constructed on a low terrace on the southern bank of the Kasu River (粕川), a branch of the Ino River, in the southern part of Gunma Prefecture. In the surrounding area, the distribution of Early Kofun period sites and kofun is known, and to the southeast lies the {{ill|Shibazaki Asama-yama Kofun|ja|柴崎浅間山古墳}}. In {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), grave goods were excavated, and subsequently the mound was leveled. The original shape of the tumulus is unclear; {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾}} regarded it as a small {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} of about 12 meters in diameter, while {{ill|Motomura Gōshō|ja|本村豪章}} considered it a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳}} with a side length of about 22 meters{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. No thatched stones or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were observed on the mound’s exterior{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. According to records, the burial facility is estimated to have been a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}; its main axis was oriented north–south, and traces of vermilion were found inside{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. During the excavation in {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), four bronze mirrors (2 {{ill|triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors|ja|内行花文鏡}}) as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} were recovered{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Notably, one of the triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors bears an inscription indicating the first year of the era "□始元年" (presumably the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始}} of the Wei, i.e. {{ill|340|ja|340}}), making it an important example of the early stage in the development of such mirrors{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The construction period is estimated to be in the latter half of the 4th century of the Early Kofun period{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Despite its small size, this kofun is significant because, in addition to yielding one of the few dated mirrors in Gunma Prefecture, it also contained four bronze mirrors as grave goods. These features—unreflected in the tumulus’s shape or map_size—suggest a connection with the Yamato state in the Kinai region, similar only to the {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳}} and the {{ill|Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|ja|長者屋敷天王山古墳}} in Gunma Prefecture{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The excavated items were designated as a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in {{ill|1989|ja|1989}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}}). == Site History == * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), grave goods were excavated{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * In {{ill|1926|ja|1926}} (the first year of the {{ill|Showa era|ja|昭和}}), {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾}} conducted a survey (at which time the mound had been leveled and converted to farmland){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * On June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}}), the excavated items were designated as a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990}} (Heisei 2), a survey was conducted on residential land in the Shimo-Ōruimachi area, but the kofun could not be confirmed (as recorded in the Takasaki City History){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. == Excavated Items == [[File:蟹沢古墳出土 三角縁同向式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Triangular-edged, same-facing Shikishinju Mirror
(National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}})}}{{small|{{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} exhibition.}}]] The grave goods excavated in {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} are as follows{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}: * Bronze mirrors: 4 ** {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}: 2 ***: One mirror is a triangular-edged, same-facing (stair-step type) Shikishinju mirror, with a diameter of 22.6 cm and a rim thickness of 1.0 cm. It bears the inscription "□始元年 陳是作鏡 自有經迷 本自𦭴師 杜地命出 壽如金石 保子□□" and is presumed to date to the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始}} ({{ill|340|ja|340}}) of the Wei, representing an early stage of triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors. Mirrors of similar type have been found at the {{ill|Moriho Kofun|ja|森尾古墳}} in Hyogo Prefecture and the {{ill|Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|ja|竹島御家老屋敷古墳}} in Yamaguchi Prefecture. ***: Another mirror is a triangular-edged mirror depicting three gods and three beasts, with a diameter of 21.9 cm and a rim thickness of 2.1 cm. Its condition is notably poor. Similar mirrors have been found at the {{ill|Niiyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture, the {{ill|Manai Kofun|ja|真名井古墳}} in Osaka Prefecture, and a site in southern Kyoto (now in the collection of the Kurashiki Archaeological Museum). ** {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡}}: 2 ***: One mirror is an eight-flower (Hakka) mirror, with a diameter of 10.14 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. ***: Another mirror is a six-flower (Rokka) mirror, with a diameter of 9.8 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. * Iron axes: 2 *: Rectangular iron axes. One piece measures 12.4 cm in length, 4.3 cm in width, and 1.0 cm in thickness; the other measures 8.4 cm in length, 4.4 cm in width, and 1.3 cm in thickness. * Iron adze: 1 *: Some sources refer to it as an iron spear. It is double-edged, with a remaining length of 18.1 cm and a blade width of 1.0 cm. * Fragments of iron swords: 4 fragmentary pieces. * Fragments of a straight sword: with a remaining length of 22.3 cm. * Fragments of {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}: 9 pieces, representing three types – a double-mouthed jar, an S-shaped jar with a rim base, and a jar with high shoulders. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Gunma Prefecture Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun Excavated Items (archaeological materials) – details are as follows. These items are held by the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} and were designated on June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}}){{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳出土品}}. ** Bronze mirrors: *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror with an inscription indicating the first year of □始元年. *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror. *** 2 Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors. ** Swords and related items: *** Fragments of iron swords – collectively. *** Fragments of iron sword remnants – collectively. ** 2 pieces of fragmented iron axes. ** 1 piece of fragmented iron halberd. ** 9 pieces of fragmented {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (located in Taitō, Tokyo) – holds the excavated items from the Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Saburō Kobayashi Kani-sawa Kofun |map_dot_label position = top }} '''Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun''' (柴崎蟹沢古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shibazaki Town, {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. Its shape was either a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} or a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳}}. The mound is no longer extant, and the excavated items have been designated as a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Overview == It is a small kofun constructed on a low terrace on the southern bank of the Kasu River (粕川), a branch of the Ino River, in the southern part of Gunma Prefecture. In the surrounding area, the distribution of Early Kofun period sites and kofun is known, and to the southeast lies the {{ill|Shibazaki Asama-yama Kofun|ja|柴崎浅間山古墳}}. In {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), grave goods were excavated, and subsequently the mound was leveled. The original shape of the tumulus is unclear; {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾}} regarded it as a small {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} of about 12 meters in diameter, while {{ill|Motomura Gōshō|ja|本村豪章}} considered it a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳}} with a side length of about 22 meters{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. No thatched stones or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were observed on the mound’s exterior{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. According to records, the burial facility is estimated to have been a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}; its main axis was oriented north–south, and traces of vermilion were found inside{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. During the excavation in {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), four bronze mirrors (2 {{ill|triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors|ja|内行花文鏡}}) as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} were recovered{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Notably, one of the triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors bears an inscription indicating the first year of the era "□始元年" (presumably the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始}} of the Wei, i.e. {{ill|340|ja|340}}), making it an important example of the early stage in the development of such mirrors{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The construction period is estimated to be in the latter half of the 4th century of the Early Kofun period{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Despite its small size, this kofun is significant because, in addition to yielding one of the few dated mirrors in Gunma Prefecture, it also contained four bronze mirrors as grave goods. These features—unreflected in the tumulus’s shape or map_size—suggest a connection with the Yamato state in the Kinai region, similar only to the {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳}} and the {{ill|Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|ja|長者屋敷天王山古墳}} in Gunma Prefecture{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The excavated items were designated as a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in {{ill|1989|ja|1989}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}}). == Site History == * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), grave goods were excavated{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * In {{ill|1926|ja|1926}} (the first year of the {{ill|Showa era|ja|昭和}}), {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾}} conducted a survey (at which time the mound had been leveled and converted to farmland){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * On June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}}), the excavated items were designated as a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990}} (Heisei 2), a survey was conducted on residential land in the Shimo-Ōruimachi area, but the kofun could not be confirmed (as recorded in the Takasaki City History){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. == Excavated Items == [[File:蟹沢古墳出土 三角縁同向式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Triangular-edged, same-facing Shikishinju Mirror
(National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}})}}{{small|{{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} exhibition.}}]] The grave goods excavated in {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} are as follows{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}: * Bronze mirrors: 4 ** {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}: 2 ***: One mirror is a triangular-edged, same-facing (stair-step type) Shikishinju mirror, with a diameter of 22.6 cm and a rim thickness of 1.0 cm. It bears the inscription "□始元年 陳是作鏡 自有經迷 本自𦭴師 杜地命出 壽如金石 保子□□" and is presumed to date to the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始}} ({{ill|340|ja|340}}) of the Wei, representing an early stage of triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors. Mirrors of similar type have been found at the {{ill|Moriho Kofun|ja|森尾古墳}} in Hyogo Prefecture and the {{ill|Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|ja|竹島御家老屋敷古墳}} in Yamaguchi Prefecture. ***: Another mirror is a triangular-edged mirror depicting three gods and three beasts, with a diameter of 21.9 cm and a rim thickness of 2.1 cm. Its condition is notably poor. Similar mirrors have been found at the {{ill|Niiyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture, the {{ill|Manai Kofun|ja|真名井古墳}} in Osaka Prefecture, and a site in southern Kyoto (now in the collection of the Kurashiki Archaeological Museum). ** {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡}}: 2 ***: One mirror is an eight-flower (Hakka) mirror, with a diameter of 10.14 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. ***: Another mirror is a six-flower (Rokka) mirror, with a diameter of 9.8 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. * Iron axes: 2 *: Rectangular iron axes. One piece measures 12.4 cm in length, 4.3 cm in width, and 1.0 cm in thickness; the other measures 8.4 cm in length, 4.4 cm in width, and 1.3 cm in thickness. * Iron adze: 1 *: Some sources refer to it as an iron spear. It is double-edged, with a remaining length of 18.1 cm and a blade width of 1.0 cm. * Fragments of iron swords: 4 fragmentary pieces. * Fragments of a straight sword: with a remaining length of 22.3 cm. * Fragments of {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}: 9 pieces, representing three types – a double-mouthed jar, an S-shaped jar with a rim base, and a jar with high shoulders. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Gunma Prefecture Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun Excavated Items (archaeological materials) – details are as follows. These items are held by the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} and were designated on June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}}){{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳出土品}}. ** Bronze mirrors: *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror with an inscription indicating the first year of □始元年. *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror. *** 2 Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors. ** Swords and related items: *** Fragments of iron swords – collectively. *** Fragments of iron sword remnants – collectively. ** 2 pieces of fragmented iron axes. ** 1 piece of fragmented iron halberd. ** 9 pieces of fragmented {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (located in Taitō, Tokyo) – holds the excavated items from the Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Saburō Kobayashi Daijiten}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|蟹沢古墳（古墳）|1989}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=|year=1999|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title=New Edited Takasaki City Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳}} |image = |alternate_names = | part_of = |Location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}{{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市}} Shibazaki Town (柴崎町) |coordinatess = 36 |coordinatess = 18 |coordinatess = 52.58 |coordinatess = 139 |coordinatess = 3 |coordinatess = 24.43 |ISO = JP-10 |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} or {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳}} |map_size = Diameter 12 m or side length 22 m |Burial Facility = (Estimated) {{ill|Clay Coffin|ja|粘土槨}} |excavations = 2 {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}s (including 1 bronze mirror with an inscription from the first year of the Seishi era) and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡}}s, as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |Secondary tumulus = |Period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |Interred = |Mausoleum = |Historic Site = None |Tangible Cultural Property = excavations (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}) |Notes = The mound is no longer extant |map = Japan Gunma |Icon = |map_dot_label = Shibazaki
Kani-sawa Kofun |map_dot_label position = top }} '''Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun''' (柴崎蟹沢古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shibazaki Town, {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. Its shape was either a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} or a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳}}. The mound is no longer extant, and the excavated items have been designated as a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Overview == It is a small kofun constructed on a low terrace on the southern bank of the Kasu River (粕川), a branch of the Ino River, in the southern part of Gunma Prefecture. In the surrounding area, the distribution of Early Kofun period sites and kofun is known, and to the southeast lies the {{ill|Shibazaki Asama-yama Kofun|ja|柴崎浅間山古墳}}. In {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), grave goods were excavated, and subsequently the mound was leveled. The original shape of the tumulus is unclear; {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾}} regarded it as a small {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} of about 12 meters in diameter, while {{ill|Motomura Gōshō|ja|本村豪章}} considered it a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳}} with a side length of about 22 meters{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. No thatched stones or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were observed on the mound’s exterior{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. According to records, the burial facility is estimated to have been a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}; its main axis was oriented north–south, and traces of vermilion were found inside{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. During the excavation in {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), four bronze mirrors (2 {{ill|triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors|ja|内行花文鏡}}) as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} were recovered{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Notably, one of the triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors bears an inscription indicating the first year of the era "□始元年" (presumably the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始}} of the Wei, i.e. {{ill|340|ja|340}}), making it an important example of the early stage in the development of such mirrors{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The construction period is estimated to be in the latter half of the 4th century of the Early Kofun period{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Despite its small size, this kofun is significant because, in addition to yielding one of the few dated mirrors in Gunma Prefecture, it also contained four bronze mirrors as grave goods. These features—unreflected in the tumulus’s shape or map_size—suggest a connection with the Yamato state in the Kinai region, similar only to the {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳}} and the {{ill|Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|ja|長者屋敷天王山古墳}} in Gunma Prefecture{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The excavated items were designated as a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in {{ill|1989|ja|1989}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}}). == Site History == * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), grave goods were excavated{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * In {{ill|1926|ja|1926}} (the first year of the {{ill|Showa era|ja|昭和}}), {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾}} conducted a survey (at which time the mound had been leveled and converted to farmland){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * On June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}}), the excavated items were designated as a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990}} (Heisei 2), a survey was conducted on residential land in the Shimo-Ōruimachi area, but the kofun could not be confirmed (as recorded in the Takasaki City History){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. == Excavated Items == [[File:蟹沢古墳出土 三角縁同向式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Triangular-edged, same-facing Shikishinju Mirror
(National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}})}}{{small|{{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} exhibition.}}]] The grave goods excavated in {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} are as follows{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}: * Bronze mirrors: 4 ** {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}: 2 ***: One mirror is a triangular-edged, same-facing (stair-step type) Shikishinju mirror, with a diameter of 22.6 cm and a rim thickness of 1.0 cm. It bears the inscription "□始元年 陳是作鏡 自有經迷 本自𦭴師 杜地命出 壽如金石 保子□□" and is presumed to date to the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始}} ({{ill|340|ja|340}}) of the Wei, representing an early stage of triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors. Mirrors of similar type have been found at the {{ill|Moriho Kofun|ja|森尾古墳}} in Hyogo Prefecture and the {{ill|Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|ja|竹島御家老屋敷古墳}} in Yamaguchi Prefecture. ***: Another mirror is a triangular-edged mirror depicting three gods and three beasts, with a diameter of 21.9 cm and a rim thickness of 2.1 cm. Its condition is notably poor. Similar mirrors have been found at the {{ill|Niiyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture, the {{ill|Manai Kofun|ja|真名井古墳}} in Osaka Prefecture, and a site in southern Kyoto (now in the collection of the Kurashiki Archaeological Museum). ** {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡}}: 2 ***: One mirror is an eight-flower (Hakka) mirror, with a diameter of 10.14 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. ***: Another mirror is a six-flower (Rokka) mirror, with a diameter of 9.8 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. * Iron axes: 2 *: Rectangular iron axes. One piece measures 12.4 cm in length, 4.3 cm in width, and 1.0 cm in thickness; the other measures 8.4 cm in length, 4.4 cm in width, and 1.3 cm in thickness. * Iron adze: 1 *: Some sources refer to it as an iron spear. It is double-edged, with a remaining length of 18.1 cm and a blade width of 1.0 cm. * Fragments of iron swords: 4 fragmentary pieces. * Fragments of a straight sword: with a remaining length of 22.3 cm. * Fragments of {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}: 9 pieces, representing three types – a double-mouthed jar, an S-shaped jar with a rim base, and a jar with high shoulders. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Gunma Prefecture Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun Excavated Items (archaeological materials) – details are as follows. These items are held by the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} and were designated on June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}}){{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳出土品}}. ** Bronze mirrors: *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror with an inscription indicating the first year of □始元年. *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror. *** 2 Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors. ** Swords and related items: *** Fragments of iron swords – collectively. *** Fragments of iron sword remnants – collectively. ** 2 pieces of fragmented iron axes. ** 1 piece of fragmented iron halberd. ** 9 pieces of fragmented {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (located in Taitō, Tokyo) – holds the excavated items from the Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Saburō Kobayashi (archaeologist)|ja|小林三郎 (考古学者)|lt=Saburō Kobayashi|lt_ja=小林三郎}}|year=1989|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|lt=Nihon Kofun Daijiten}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|蟹沢古墳（古墳）|1989}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=|year=1999|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title=New Edited Takasaki City History|volume=Materials Edition 1: Early Ancient I|publisher={{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Bronze mirror#Inscriptions|ja|銅鏡#紀年銘鏡}} Its condition is notably poor. Similar mirrors have been found at the {{ill|Niiyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture, the {{ill|Manai Kofun|ja|真名井古墳}} in Osaka Prefecture, and a site in southern Kyoto (now in the collection of the Kurashiki Archaeological Museum). ** {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡}}: 2 ***: One mirror is an eight-flower (Hakka) mirror, with a diameter of 10.14 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. ***: Another mirror is a six-flower (Rokka) mirror, with a diameter of 9.8 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. * Iron axes: 2 *: Rectangular iron axes. One piece measures 12.4 cm in length, 4.3 cm in width, and 1.0 cm in thickness; the other measures 8.4 cm in length, 4.4 cm in width, and 1.3 cm in thickness. * Iron adze: 1 *: Some sources refer to it as an iron spear. It is double-edged, with a remaining length of 18.1 cm and a blade width of 1.0 cm. * Fragments of iron swords: 4 fragmentary pieces. * Fragments of a straight sword: with a remaining length of 22.3 cm. * Fragments of {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}: 9 pieces, representing three types – a double-mouthed jar, an S-typed jar with a rim base, and a jar with high shoulders. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Gunma Prefecture Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun Excavated Items (archaeological materials) – details are as follows. These items are held by the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} and were designated on June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}}){{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳出土品}}. ** Bronze mirrors: *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror with an inscription indicating the first year of □始元年. *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror. *** 2 Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors. ** Swords and related items: *** Fragments of iron swords – collectively. *** Fragments of iron sword remnants – collectively. ** 2 pieces of fragmented iron axes. ** 1 piece of fragmented iron halberd. ** 9 pieces of fragmented {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (located in Taitō, Tokyo) – holds the excavated items from the Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Saburō Kobayashi from the first year of the Seishi era) and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡}}s, as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |Secondary tumulus = |Period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |Interred = |Mausoleum = |Historic Site = None |Tangible Cultural Property = excavations (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}) |Notes = The mound is no longer extant |map = Japan Gunma |Icon = |map_dot_label = Shibazaki
Kani-sawa Kofun |map_dot_label position = top }} '''Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun''' (柴崎蟹沢古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shibazaki Town, {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. Its type was either a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} or a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳}}. The mound is no longer extant, and the excavated items have been designated as a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Overview == It is a small kofun constructed on a low terrace on the southern bank of the Kasu River (粕川), a branch of the Ino River, in the southern part of Gunma Prefecture. In the surrounding area, the distribution of Early Kofun period sites and kofun is known, and to the southeast lies the {{ill|Shibazaki Asama-yama Kofun|ja|柴崎浅間山古墳}}. In {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), grave goods were excavated, and subsequently the mound was leveled. The original type of the tumulus is unclear; {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾}} regarded it as a small {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} of about 12 meters in diameter, while {{ill|Motomura Gōshō|ja|本村豪章}} considered it a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳}} with a side length of about 22 meters{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. No thatched stones or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were observed on the mound’s exterior{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. According to records, the burial facility is estimated to have been a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}; its main axis was oriented north–south, and traces of vermilion were found inside{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. During the excavation in {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), four bronze mirrors (2 {{ill|triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors|ja|内行花文鏡}}) as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} were recovered{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Notably, one of the triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors bears an inscription indicating the first year of the era "□始元年" (presumably the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始}} of the Wei, i.e. {{ill|340|ja|340}}), making it an important example of the early stage in the development of such mirrors{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The construction period is estimated to be in the latter half of the 4th century of the Early Kofun period{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Despite its small size, this kofun is significant because, in addition to yielding one of the few dated mirrors in Gunma Prefecture, it also contained four bronze mirrors as grave goods. These features—unreflected in the tumulus’s type or map_size—suggest a connection with the Yamato state in the Kinai region, similar only to the {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳}} and the {{ill|Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|ja|長者屋敷天王山古墳}} in Gunma Prefecture{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The excavated items were designated as a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in {{ill|1989|ja|1989}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}}). == Site History == * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), grave goods were excavated{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * In {{ill|1926|ja|1926}} (the first year of the {{ill|Showa era|ja|昭和}}), {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾}} conducted a survey (at which time the mound had been leveled and converted to farmland){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * On June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}}), the excavated items were designated as a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990}} (Heisei 2), a survey was conducted on residential land in the Shimo-Ōruimachi area, but the kofun could not be confirmed (as recorded in the Takasaki City History){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. == Excavated Items == [[File:蟹沢古墳出土 三角縁同向式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Triangular-edged, same-facing Shikishinju Mirror
(National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}})}}{{small|{{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} exhibition.}}]] The grave goods excavated in {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} are as follows{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}: * Bronze mirrors: 4 ** {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}: 2 ***: One mirror is a triangular-edged, same-facing (stair-step type) Shikishinju mirror, with a diameter of 22.6 cm and a rim thickness of 1.0 cm. It bears the inscription "□始元年 陳是作鏡 自有經迷 本自𦭴師 杜地命出 壽如金石 保子□□" and is presumed to date to the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始}} ({{ill|340|ja|340}}) of the Wei, representing an early stage of triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors. Mirrors of similar type have been found at the {{ill|Moriho Kofun|ja|森尾古墳}} in Hyogo Prefecture and the {{ill|Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|ja|竹島御家老屋敷古墳}} in Yamaguchi Prefecture. ***: Another mirror is a triangular-edged mirror depicting three gods and three beasts, with a diameter of 21.9 cm and a rim thickness of 2.1 cm. Its condition is notably poor. Similar mirrors have been found at the {{ill|Niiyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture, the {{ill|Manai Kofun|ja|真名井古墳}} in Osaka Prefecture, and a site in southern Kyoto (now in the collection of the Kurashiki Archaeological Museum). ** {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡}}: 2 ***: One mirror is an eight-flower (Hakka) mirror, with a diameter of 10.14 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. ***: Another mirror is a six-flower (Rokka) mirror, with a diameter of 9.8 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. * Iron axes: 2 *: Rectangular iron axes. One piece measures 12.4 cm in length, 4.3 cm in width, and 1.0 cm in thickness; the other measures 8.4 cm in length, 4.4 cm in width, and 1.3 cm in thickness. * Iron adze: 1 *: Some sources refer to it as an iron spear. It is double-edged, with a remaining length of 18.1 cm and a blade width of 1.0 cm. * Fragments of iron swords: 4 fragmentary pieces. * Fragments of a straight sword: with a remaining length of 22.3 cm. * Fragments of {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}: 9 pieces, representing three types – a double-mouthed jar, an S-shaped jar with a rim base, and a jar with high shoulders. == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Gunma Prefecture Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun Excavated Items (archaeological materials) – details are as follows. These items are held by the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} and were designated on June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}}){{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳出土品}}. ** Bronze mirrors: *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror with an inscription indicating the first year of □始元年. *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror. *** 2 Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors. ** Swords and related items: *** Fragments of iron swords – collectively. *** Fragments of iron sword remnants – collectively. ** 2 pieces of fragmented iron axes. ** 1 piece of fragmented iron halberd. ** 9 pieces of fragmented {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (located in Taitō, Tokyo) – holds the excavated items from the Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Saburō Kobayashi (archaeologist)|ja|小林三郎 (考古学者)|lt=Saburō Kobayashi|lt_ja=小林三郎}}|year=1989|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|lt=Nihon Kofun Daijiten}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|蟹沢古墳（古墳）|1989}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=|year=1999|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title=New Edited Takasaki City History|volume=Materials Edition 1: Early Ancient I|publisher={{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Bronze mirror#Inscriptions|ja|銅鏡#紀年銘鏡}} According to records, the burial facility is estimated to have been a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}; its main axis was oriented north–south, and traces of vermilion were found inside{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. During the excavation in {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), four bronze mirrors (2 {{ill|triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors|ja|内行花文鏡}}) as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} were recovered{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Notably, one of the triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors bears an inscription indicating the first year of the era "□始元年" (presumably the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始}} of the Wei, i.e. {{ill|340|ja|340}}), making it an important example of the early stage in the development of such mirrors{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The construction period is estimated to be in the latter half of the 4th century of the Early Kofun period{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Despite its small size, this kofun is significant because, in addition to yielding one of the few dated mirrors in Gunma Prefecture, it also contained four bronze mirrors as grave goods. These features—unreflected in the tumulus’s type or map_size—suggest a connection with the Yamato state in the Kinai region, similar only to the {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳}} and the {{ill|Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|ja|長者屋敷天王山古墳}} in Gunma Prefecture{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The excavated items were designated as a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in {{ill|1989|ja|1989}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}}). == Site History == * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), grave goods were excavated{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * In {{ill|1926|ja|1926}} (the first year of the {{ill|Showa era|ja|昭和}}), {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾}} conducted a survey (at which time the mound had been leveled and converted to farmland){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * On June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}}), the excavated items were designated as a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990}} (Heisei 2), a survey was conducted on residential land in the Shimo-Ōruimachi area, but the kofun could not be confirmed (as recorded in the Takasaki City History){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. == Excavated Items == [[File:蟹沢古墳出土 三角縁同向式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Triangular-edged, same-facing Shikishinju Mirror
(National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}})}}{{small|{{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} exhibition.}}]] The grave goods excavated in {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} are as follows{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}: * Bronze mirrors: 4 ** {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}: 2 ***: One mirror is a triangular-edged, same-facing (stair-step type) Shikishinju mirror, with a diameter of 22.6 cm and a rim thickness of 1.0 cm. It bears the inscription "□始元年 陳是作鏡 自有經迷 本自𦭴師 杜地命出 壽如金石 保子□□" and is presumed to date to the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始}} ({{ill|340|ja|340}}) of the Wei, representing an early stage of triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors. Mirrors of similar type have been found at the {{ill|Moriho Kofun|ja|森尾古墳}} in Hyogo Prefecture and the {{ill|Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|ja|竹島御家老屋敷古墳}} in Yamaguchi Prefecture. ***: Another mirror is a triangular-edged mirror depicting three gods and three beasts, with a diameter of 21.9 cm and a rim thickness of 2.1 cm. Its condition is notably poor. Similar mirrors have been found at the {{ill|Niiyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture, the {{ill|Manai Kofun|ja|真名井古墳}} in Osaka Prefecture, and a site in southern Kyoto (now in the collection of the Kurashiki Archaeological Museum). ** {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡}}: 2 ***: One mirror is an eight-flower (Hakka) mirror, with a diameter of 10.14 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. ***: Another mirror is a six-flower (Rokka) mirror, with a diameter of 9.8 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. * Iron axes: 2 *: Rectangular iron axes. One piece measures 12.4 cm in length, 4.3 cm in width, and 1.0 cm in thickness; the other measures 8.4 cm in length, 4.4 cm in width, and 1.3 cm in thickness. * Iron adze: 1 *: Some sources refer to it as an iron spear. It is double-edged, with a remaining length of 18.1 cm and a blade width of 1.0 cm. * Fragments of iron swords: 4 fragmentary pieces. * Fragments of a straight sword: with a remaining length of 22.3 cm. * Fragments of {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}: 9 pieces, representing three types – a double-mouthed jar, an S-shaped jar with a rim base, and a jar with high shoulders. == other_designation == === National Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Gunma Prefecture Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun Excavated Items (archaeological materials) – details are as follows. These items are held by the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} and were designated on June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}}){{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳出土品}}. ** Bronze mirrors: *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror with an inscription indicating the first year of □始元年. *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror. *** 2 Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors. ** Swords and related items: *** Fragments of iron swords – collectively. *** Fragments of iron sword remnants – collectively. ** 2 pieces of fragmented iron axes. ** 1 piece of fragmented iron halberd. ** 9 pieces of fragmented {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (located in Taitō, Tokyo) – holds the excavated items from the Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Saburō Kobayashi (archaeologist)|ja|小林三郎 (考古学者)|lt=Saburō Kobayashi|lt_ja=小林三郎}}|year=1989|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|lt=Nihon Kofun Daijiten}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|蟹沢古墳（古墳）|1989}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=|year=1999|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title=New Edited Takasaki City History|volume=Materials Edition 1: Early Ancient I|publisher={{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Bronze mirror#Inscriptions|ja|銅鏡#紀年銘鏡}} == External Links == * {{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳出土品}} {{citation needed|しはさきかにさわこふん}} [[Category:Ancient Tombs in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takasaki City]] [[Category:National |coordinatess = 52.58 |coordinatess = 139 |coordinatess = 3 |coordinatess = 24.43 |ISO = JP-10 |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} or {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳}} |map_size = Diameter 12 m or side length 22 m |burial = (Estimated) {{ill|Clay Coffin|ja|粘土槨}} |excavations = 2 {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}s (including 1 bronze mirror with an inscription from the first year of the Seishi era) and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡}}s, as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} |Secondary tumulus = |Period = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} |Interred = |Mausoleum = |Historic Site = None |Tangible Cultural Property = excavations (National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}) |Notes = The mound is no longer extant |map = Japan Gunma |Icon = |map_dot_label = Shibazaki
Kani-sawa Kofun |map_dot_label position = top }} '''Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun''' (柴崎蟹沢古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in Shibazaki Town, {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. Its type was either a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} or a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳}}. The mound is no longer extant, and the burial goods have been designated as a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Overview == It is a small kofun constructed on a low terrace on the southern bank of the Kasu River (粕川), a branch of the Ino River, in the southern part of Gunma Prefecture. In the surrounding area, the distribution of Early Kofun period sites and kofun is known, and to the southeast lies the {{ill|Shibazaki Asama-yama Kofun|ja|柴崎浅間山古墳}}. In {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), grave goods were excavated, and subsequently the mound was leveled. The original type of the tumulus is unclear; {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾}} regarded it as a small {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} of about 12 meters in diameter, while {{ill|Motomura Gōshō|ja|本村豪章}} considered it a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳}} with a side length of about 22 meters{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. No thatched stones or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were observed on the mound’s exterior{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. According to records, the burial facility is estimated to have been a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨}}{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}; its main axis was oriented north–south, and traces of vermilion were found inside{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. During the excavation in {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), four bronze mirrors (2 {{ill|triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors|ja|内行花文鏡}}) as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} were recovered{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Notably, one of the triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors bears an inscription indicating the first year of the era "□始元年" (presumably the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始}} of the Wei, i.e. {{ill|340|ja|340}}), making it an important example of the early stage in the development of such mirrors{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The construction period is estimated to be in the latter half of the 4th century of the Early Kofun period{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Despite its small size, this kofun is significant because, in addition to yielding one of the few dated mirrors in Gunma Prefecture, it also contained four bronze mirrors as grave goods. These features—unreflected in the tumulus’s type or map_size—suggest a connection with the Yamato state in the Kinai region, similar only to the {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳}} and the {{ill|Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|ja|長者屋敷天王山古墳}} in Gunma Prefecture{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The burial goods were designated as a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} in {{ill|1989|ja|1989}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}}). == Site History == * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治}}), grave goods were excavated{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * In {{ill|1926|ja|1926}} (the first year of the {{ill|Showa era|ja|昭和}}), {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾}} conducted a survey (at which time the mound had been leveled and converted to farmland){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * On June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}}), the burial goods were designated as a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990}} (Heisei 2), a survey was conducted on residential land in the Shimo-Ōruimachi area, but the kofun could not be confirmed (as recorded in the Takasaki City History){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. == Excavated Items == [[File:蟹沢古墳出土 三角縁同向式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Triangular-edged, same-facing Shikishinju Mirror
(National {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}})}}{{small|{{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} exhibition.}}]] The grave goods excavated in {{ill|1909|ja|1909}} are as follows{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}: * Bronze mirrors: 4 ** {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}: 2 ***: One mirror is a triangular-edged, same-facing (stair-step type) Shikishinju mirror, with a diameter of 22.6 cm and a rim thickness of 1.0 cm. It bears the inscription "□始元年 陳是作鏡 自有經迷 本自𦭴師 杜地命出 壽如金石 保子□□" and is presumed to date to the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始}} ({{ill|340|ja|340}}) of the Wei, representing an early stage of triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors. Mirrors of similar type have been found at the {{ill|Moriho Kofun|ja|森尾古墳}} in Hyogo Prefecture and the {{ill|Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|ja|竹島御家老屋敷古墳}} in Yamaguchi Prefecture. ***: Another mirror is a triangular-edged mirror depicting three gods and three beasts, with a diameter of 21.9 cm and a rim thickness of 2.1 cm. Its condition is notably poor. Similar mirrors have been found at the {{ill|Niiyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳}} in Nara Prefecture, the {{ill|Manai Kofun|ja|真名井古墳}} in Osaka Prefecture, and a site in southern Kyoto (now in the collection of the Kurashiki Archaeological Museum). ** {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡}}: 2 ***: One mirror is an eight-flower (Hakka) mirror, with a diameter of 10.14 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. ***: Another mirror is a six-flower (Rokka) mirror, with a diameter of 9.8 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. * Iron axes: 2 *: Rectangular iron axes. One piece measures 12.4 cm in length, 4.3 cm in width, and 1.0 cm in thickness; the other measures 8.4 cm in length, 4.4 cm in width, and 1.3 cm in thickness. * Iron adze: 1 *: Some sources refer to it as an iron spear. It is double-edged, with a remaining length of 18.1 cm and a blade width of 1.0 cm. * Fragments of iron swords: 4 fragmentary pieces. * Fragments of a straight sword: with a remaining length of 22.3 cm. * Fragments of {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}: 9 pieces, representing three types – a double-mouthed jar, an S-shaped jar with a rim base, and a jar with high shoulders. == other_designation == === National Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Gunma Prefecture Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun Excavated Items (archaeological materials) – details are as follows. These items are held by the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} and were designated on June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成}}){{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳出土品}}. ** Bronze mirrors: *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror with an inscription indicating the first year of □始元年. *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror. *** 2 Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors. ** Swords and related items: *** Fragments of iron swords – collectively. *** Fragments of iron sword remnants – collectively. ** 2 pieces of fragmented iron axes. ** 1 piece of fragmented iron halberd. ** 9 pieces of fragmented {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} (located in Taitō, Tokyo) – holds the burial goods from the Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Saburō Kobayashi (archaeologist)|ja|小林三郎 (考古学者)|lt=Saburō Kobayashi|lt_ja=小林三郎}}|year=1989|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|lt=Nihon Kofun Diameter 12 m or side length 22 m |burial = (Estimated) {{ill|Clay Coffin|ja|粘土槨}} |excavations = 2 {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}s (including 1 bronze mirror with an
