revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = {{ill|Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} |image = |alternate_name = | part_of = |location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|12=draft|12=draft}} Shibazaki Town (柴崎町) |coordinates = 36 |coordinates = 18 |coordinates = 52.58 |coordinates = 139 |coordinates = 3 |coordinates = 24.43 |omitted = JP-10 |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} |map_size = Diameter 12 m or side length 22 m |burial = (Estimated) {{ill|Clay Coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} |excavations = 2 {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|12=draft|12=draft}}s (including 1 bronze mirror with an inscription from the first year of the Seishi era) and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}s, as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} |Secondary tumulus = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |Interred = |Mausoleum = |Historic Site = None |Tangible designation1 = excavations (National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}) |notes = The mound is no longer extant |map = Japan Gunma |type = |map_dot_label = Shibazaki
Kani-sawa Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun''' (柴崎蟹沢古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Shibazaki Town, {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its type was either a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} or a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound is no longer extant, and the burial goods have been Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == It is a small kofun constructed on a low terrace on the southern bank of the Kasu River (粕川), a branch of the Ino River, in the southern part of Gunma Prefecture. In the surrounding area, the distribution of Early Kofun period sites and kofun is known, and to the southeast lies the {{ill|Shibazaki Asama-yama Kofun|ja|柴崎浅間山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}. In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|12=draft|12=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}}), grave goods were excavated, and subsequently the mound was leveled. The original type of the tumulus is unclear; {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|12=draft|12=draft}} regarded it as a small {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} of about 12 meters in diameter, while {{ill|Motomura Gōshō|ja|本村豪章|12=draft|12=draft}} considered it a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} with a side length of about 22 meters{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. No thatched stones or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} were observed on the mound’s exterior{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. According to records, the burial facility is estimated to have been a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}; its main axis was oriented north–south, and traces of vermilion were found inside{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. During the excavation in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|12=draft|12=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}}), four bronze mirrors (2 {{ill|triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}) as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} were recovered{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Notably, one of the triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors bears an inscription indicating the first year of the era "□始元年" (presumably the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|12=draft|12=draft}} of the Wei, i.e. {{ill|340|ja|340|12=draft|12=draft}}), making it an important example of the early stage in the development of such mirrors{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The construction period is estimated to be in the latter half of the 4th century of the Early Kofun period{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Despite its small size, this kofun is significant because, in addition to yielding one of the few dated mirrors in Gunma Prefecture, it also contained four bronze mirrors as grave goods. These features—unreflected in the tumulus’s type or map_size—suggest a connection with the Yamato state in the Kinai region, similar only to the {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|ja|長者屋敷天王山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Gunma Prefecture{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|1989|ja|1989|12=draft|12=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Site History == * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|12=draft|12=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}}), grave goods were excavated{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * In {{ill|1926|ja|1926|12=draft|12=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Showa era|ja|昭和|12=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|12=draft|12=draft}} conducted a survey (at which time the mound had been leveled and converted to farmland){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * On June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|12=draft|12=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|12=draft|12=draft}}), the burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990|12=draft|12=draft}} (Heisei 2), a survey was conducted on residential land in the Shimo-Ōruimachi area, but the kofun could not be confirmed (as recorded in the Takasaki City History){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. == burial goods == [[File:蟹沢古墳出土 三角縁同向式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Triangular-edged, same-facing Shikishinju Mirror
(National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}})}}{{small|{{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} exhibition.}}]] The grave goods excavated in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|12=draft|12=draft}} are as follows{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}: * Bronze mirrors: 4 ** {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|12=draft|12=draft}}: 2 ***: One mirror is a triangular-edged, same-facing (stair-step type) Shikishinju mirror, with a diameter of 22.6 cm and a rim thickness of 1.0 cm. It bears the inscription "□始元年 陳是作鏡 自有經迷 本自𦭴師 杜地命出 壽如金石 保子□□" and is presumed to date to the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|340|ja|340|12=draft|12=draft}}) of the Wei, representing an early stage of triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors. Mirrors of similar type have been found at the {{ill|Moriho Kofun|ja|森尾古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Hyogo Prefecture and the {{ill|Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|ja|竹島御家老屋敷古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Yamaguchi Prefecture. ***: Another mirror is a triangular-edged mirror depicting three gods and three beasts, with a diameter of 21.9 cm and a rim thickness of 2.1 cm. Its condition is notably poor. Similar mirrors have been found at the {{ill|Niiyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Nara Prefecture, the {{ill|Manai Kofun|ja|真名井古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Osaka Prefecture, and a site in southern Kyoto (now in the collection of the Kurashiki Archaeological Museum). ** {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}: 2 ***: One mirror is an eight-flower (Hakka) mirror, with a diameter of 10.14 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. ***: Another mirror is a six-flower (Rokka) mirror, with a diameter of 9.8 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. * Iron axes: 2 *: Rectangular iron axes. One piece measures 12.4 cm in length, 4.3 cm in width, and 1.0 cm in thickness; the other measures 8.4 cm in length, 4.4 cm in width, and 1.3 cm in thickness. * Iron adze: 1 *: Some sources refer to it as an iron spear. It is double-edged, with a remaining length of 18.1 cm and a blade width of 1.0 cm. * Fragments of iron swords: 4 fragmentary pieces. * Fragments of a straight sword: with a remaining length of 22.3 cm. * Fragments of {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}: 9 pieces, representing three types – a double-mouthed jar, an S-shaped jar with a rim base, and a jar with high shoulders. == Historic Site == === National Important designation1 (National designation1) === * Gunma Prefecture Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun burial goods (archaeological materials) – details are as follows. These items are held by the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} and were Historic Site1 on June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|12=draft|12=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|12=draft|12=draft}}){{国cultural property等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳Excavated Artifacts}}. ** Bronze mirrors: *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror with an inscription indicating the first year of □始元年. *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror. *** 2 Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors. ** Swords and related items: *** Fragments of iron swords – collectively. *** Fragments of iron sword remnants – collectively. ** 2 pieces of fragmented iron axes. ** 1 piece of fragmented iron halberd. ** 9 pieces of fragmented {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} (located in Taitō, Tokyo) – holds the burial goods from the Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Saburō Kobayashi (archaeologist)|ja|小林三郎 (考古学者)|lt=Saburō Kobayashi|lt_ja=小林三郎|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=1989|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|lt=Nihon Kofun Daijiten|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = {{ill|Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} |image = |alternate_name = | part_of = |location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|12=draft|12=draft}} Shibazaki Town (柴崎町) |coordinates = 36 |coordinates = 18 |coordinates = 52.58 |coordinates = 139 |coordinates = 3 |coordinates = 24.43 |omitted = JP-10 |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} |map_size = Diameter 12 m or side length 22 m |burial = (Estimated) {{ill|Clay Coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} |excavations = 2 {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|12=draft|12=draft}}s (including 1 bronze mirror with an inscription from the first year of the Seishi era) and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}s, as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} |Secondary tumulus = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |Interred = |Mausoleum = |Historic Site = None |Tangible designation1 = excavations (National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}) |notes = The mound is no longer extant |map = Japan Gunma |type = |map_dot_label = Shibazaki
Kani-sawa Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun''' (柴崎蟹沢古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Shibazaki Town, {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its type was either a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} or a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound is no longer extant, and the burial goods have been Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == It is a small kofun constructed on a low terrace on the southern bank of the Kasu River (粕川), a branch of the Ino River, in the southern part of Gunma Prefecture. In the surrounding area, the distribution of Early Kofun period sites and kofun is known, and to the southeast lies the {{ill|Shibazaki Asama-yama Kofun|ja|柴崎浅間山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}. In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|12=draft|12=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}}), grave goods were excavated, and subsequently the mound was leveled. The original type of the tumulus is unclear; {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|12=draft|12=draft}} regarded it as a small {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} of about 12 meters in diameter, while {{ill|Motomura Gōshō|ja|本村豪章|12=draft|12=draft}} considered it a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} with a side length of about 22 meters{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. No thatched stones or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} were observed on the mound’s exterior{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. According to records, the burial facility is estimated to have been a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}; its main axis was oriented north–south, and traces of vermilion were found inside{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. During the excavation in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|12=draft|12=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}}), four bronze mirrors (2 {{ill|triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}) as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} were recovered{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Notably, one of the triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors bears an inscription indicating the first year of the era "□始元年" (presumably the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|12=draft|12=draft}} of the Wei, i.e. {{ill|340|ja|340|12=draft|12=draft}}), making it an important example of the early stage in the development of such mirrors{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The construction period is estimated to be in the latter half of the 4th century of the Early Kofun period{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Despite its small size, this kofun is significant because, in addition to yielding one of the few dated mirrors in Gunma Prefecture, it also contained four bronze mirrors as grave goods. These features—unreflected in the tumulus’s type or map_size—suggest a connection with the Yamato state in the Kinai region, similar only to the {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|ja|長者屋敷天王山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Gunma Prefecture{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|1989|ja|1989|12=draft|12=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Site History == * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|12=draft|12=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}}), grave goods were excavated{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * In {{ill|1926|ja|1926|12=draft|12=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Showa era|ja|昭和|12=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|12=draft|12=draft}} conducted a survey (at which time the mound had been leveled and converted to farmland){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * On June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|12=draft|12=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|12=draft|12=draft}}), the burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990|12=draft|12=draft}} (Heisei 2), a survey was conducted on residential land in the Shimo-Ōruimachi area, but the kofun could not be confirmed (as recorded in the Takasaki City History){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. == burial goods == [[File:蟹沢古墳出土 三角縁同向式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Triangular-edged, same-facing Shikishinju Mirror
(National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}})}}{{small|{{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} exhibition.}}]] The grave goods excavated in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|12=draft|12=draft}} are as follows{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}: * Bronze mirrors: 4 ** {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|12=draft|12=draft}}: 2 ***: One mirror is a triangular-edged, same-facing (stair-step type) Shikishinju mirror, with a diameter of 22.6 cm and a rim thickness of 1.0 cm. It bears the inscription "□始元年 陳是作鏡 自有經迷 本自𦭴師 杜地命出 壽如金石 保子□□" and is presumed to date to the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|340|ja|340|12=draft|12=draft}}) of the Wei, representing an early stage of triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors. Mirrors of similar type have been found at the {{ill|Moriho Kofun|ja|森尾古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Hyogo Prefecture and the {{ill|Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|ja|竹島御家老屋敷古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Yamaguchi Prefecture. ***: Another mirror is a triangular-edged mirror depicting three gods and three beasts, with a diameter of 21.9 cm and a rim thickness of 2.1 cm. Its condition is notably poor. Similar mirrors have been found at the {{ill|Niiyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Nara Prefecture, the {{ill|Manai Kofun|ja|真名井古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Osaka Prefecture, and a site in southern Kyoto (now in the collection of the Kurashiki Archaeological Museum). ** {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}: 2 ***: One mirror is an eight-flower (Hakka) mirror, with a diameter of 10.14 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. ***: Another mirror is a six-flower (Rokka) mirror, with a diameter of 9.8 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. * Iron axes: 2 *: Rectangular iron axes. One piece measures 12.4 cm in length, 4.3 cm in width, and 1.0 cm in thickness; the other measures 8.4 cm in length, 4.4 cm in width, and 1.3 cm in thickness. * Iron adze: 1 *: Some sources refer to it as an iron spear. It is double-edged, with a remaining length of 18.1 cm and a blade width of 1.0 cm. * Fragments of iron swords: 4 fragmentary pieces. * Fragments of a straight sword: with a remaining length of 22.3 cm. * Fragments of {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}: 9 pieces, representing three types – a double-mouthed jar, an S-shaped jar with a rim base, and a jar with high shoulders. == Historic Site == === National Important designation1 (National designation1) === * Gunma Prefecture Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun burial goods (archaeological materials) – details are as follows. These items are held by the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} and were Historic Site1 on June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|12=draft|12=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|12=draft|12=draft}}){{国cultural property等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳Excavated Artifacts}}. ** Bronze mirrors: *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror with an inscription indicating the first year of □始元年. *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror. *** 2 Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors. ** Swords and related items: *** Fragments of iron swords – collectively. *** Fragments of iron sword remnants – collectively. ** 2 pieces of fragmented iron axes. ** 1 piece of fragmented iron halberd. ** 9 pieces of fragmented {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} (located in Taitō, Tokyo) – holds the burial goods from the Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|lt=Nihon Kofun Daijiten|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|蟹沢古墳（古墳）|1989}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=|year=1999|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title=New Edited Takasaki City History|volume=Materials Edition 1: Early Ancient I|publisher={{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Bronze Its type was either a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} or a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound is no longer extant, and the burial goods have been Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == It is a small kofun constructed on a low terrace on the southern bank of the Kasu River (粕川), a branch of the Ino River, in the southern part of Gunma Prefecture. In the surrounding area, the distribution of Early Kofun period sites and kofun is known, and to the southeast lies the {{ill|Shibazaki Asama-yama Kofun|ja|柴崎浅間山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}. In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|12=draft|12=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}}), grave goods were excavated, and subsequently the mound was leveled. The original type of the tumulus is unclear; {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|12=draft|12=draft}} regarded it as a small {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} of about 12 meters in diameter, while {{ill|Motomura Gōshō|ja|本村豪章|12=draft|12=draft}} considered it a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} with a side length of about 22 meters{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. No thatched stones or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} were observed on the mound’s exterior{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. According to records, the burial facility is estimated to have been a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}; its main axis was oriented north–south, and traces of vermilion were found inside{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. During the excavation in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|12=draft|12=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}}), four bronze mirrors (2 {{ill|triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}) as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} were recovered{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Notably, one of the triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors bears an inscription indicating the first year of the era "□始元年" (presumably the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|12=draft|12=draft}} of the Wei, i.e. {{ill|340|ja|340|12=draft|12=draft}}), making it an important example of the early stage in the development of such mirrors{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The construction period is estimated to be in the latter half of the 4th century of the Early Kofun period{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Despite its small size, this kofun is significant because, in addition to yielding one of the few dated mirrors in Gunma Prefecture, it also contained four bronze mirrors as grave goods. These features—unreflected in the tumulus’s type or map_size—suggest a connection with the Yamato state in the Kinai region, similar only to the {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|ja|長者屋敷天王山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Gunma Prefecture{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|1989|ja|1989|12=draft|12=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Site History == * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|12=draft|12=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}}), grave goods were excavated{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * In {{ill|1926|ja|1926|12=draft|12=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Showa era|ja|昭和|12=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|12=draft|12=draft}} conducted a survey (at which time the mound had been leveled and converted to farmland){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * On June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|12=draft|12=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|12=draft|12=draft}}), the burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990|12=draft|12=draft}} (Heisei 2), a survey was conducted on residential land in the Shimo-Ōruimachi area, but the kofun could not be confirmed (as recorded in the Takasaki City History){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. == burial goods == [[File:蟹沢古墳出土 三角縁同向式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Triangular-edged, same-facing Shikishinju Mirror
(National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}})}}{{small|{{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} exhibition.}}]] The grave goods excavated in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|12=draft|12=draft}} are as follows{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}: * Bronze mirrors: 4 ** {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|12=draft|12=draft}}: 2 ***: One mirror is a triangular-edged, same-facing (stair-step type) Shikishinju mirror, with a diameter of 22.6 cm and a rim thickness of 1.0 cm. It bears the inscription "□始元年 陳是作鏡 自有經迷 本自𦭴師 杜地命出 壽如金石 保子□□" and is presumed to date to the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|340|ja|340|12=draft|12=draft}}) of the Wei, representing an early stage of triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors. Mirrors of similar type have been found at the {{ill|Moriho Kofun|ja|森尾古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Hyogo Prefecture and the {{ill|Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|ja|竹島御家老屋敷古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Yamaguchi Prefecture. ***: Another mirror is a triangular-edged mirror depicting three gods and three beasts, with a diameter of 21.9 cm and a rim thickness of 2.1 cm. Its condition is notably poor. Similar mirrors have been found at the {{ill|Niiyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Nara Prefecture, the {{ill|Manai Kofun|ja|真名井古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Osaka Prefecture, and a site in southern Kyoto (now in the collection of the Kurashiki Archaeological Museum). ** {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}: 2 ***: One mirror is an eight-flower (Hakka) mirror, with a diameter of 10.14 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. ***: Another mirror is a six-flower (Rokka) mirror, with a diameter of 9.8 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. * Iron axes: 2 *: Rectangular iron axes. One piece measures 12.4 cm in length, 4.3 cm in width, and 1.0 cm in thickness; the other measures 8.4 cm in length, 4.4 cm in width, and 1.3 cm in thickness. * Iron adze: 1 *: Some sources refer to it as an iron spear. It is double-edged, with a remaining length of 18.1 cm and a blade width of 1.0 cm. * Fragments of iron swords: 4 fragmentary pieces. * Fragments of a straight sword: with a remaining length of 22.3 cm. * Fragments of {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}: 9 pieces, representing three types – a double-mouthed jar, an S-shaped jar with a rim base, and a jar with high shoulders. == Historic Site == === National Important designation1 (National designation1) === * Gunma Prefecture Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun burial goods (archaeological materials) – details are as follows. These items are held by the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} and were Historic Site1 on June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|12=draft|12=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|12=draft|12=draft}}){{国cultural property等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳Excavated Artifacts}}. ** Bronze mirrors: *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror with an inscription indicating the first year of □始元年. *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror. *** 2 Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors. ** Swords and related items: *** Fragments of iron swords – collectively. *** Fragments of iron sword remnants – collectively. ** 2 pieces of fragmented iron axes. ** 1 piece of fragmented iron halberd. ** 9 pieces of fragmented {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} (located in Taitō, Tokyo) – holds the burial goods from the Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Saburō Kobayashi (archaeologist)|ja|小林三郎 (考古学者)|lt=Saburō Kobayashi|lt_ja=小林三郎|12=draft|12=draft}}|year=1989|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|lt=Nihon Kofun Daijiten|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|蟹沢古墳（古墳）|1989}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=|year=1999|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title=New Edited Takasaki City History|volume=Materials Edition 1: Early Ancient I|publisher={{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Bronze mirror#Inscriptions|ja|銅鏡#紀年銘鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{国cultural property等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳Excavated Artifacts}} {{citation needed|しはさきかにさわこふん}} of Tokyo]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages t]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:ted]] [[Category:Ancient Tombs in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takasaki City]] [[Category:National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} [[Category:translated pages]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} {{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = {{ill|Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} |image = |alternate_name = | part_of = |location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|12=draft|12=draft}} Shibazaki Town (柴崎町) |coordinates = 36 |coordinates = 18 |coordinates = 52.58 |coordinates = 139 |coordinates = 3 |coordinates = 24.43 |omitted = JP-10 |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} |map_size = Diameter 12 m or side length 22 m |burial = (Estimated) {{ill|Clay Coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} |excavations = 2 {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|12=draft|12=draft}}s (including 1 bronze mirror with an inscription from the first year of the Seishi era) and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}s, as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} |Secondary tumulus = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |Interred = |Mausoleum = |Historic Site = None |Tangible designation1 = excavations (National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}) |notes = The mound is no longer extant |map = Japan Gunma |type = |map_dot_label = Shibazaki
Kani-sawa Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun''' (柴崎蟹沢古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Shibazaki Town, {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its type was either a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} or a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound is no longer extant, and the burial goods have been Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == It is a small kofun constructed on a low terrace on the southern bank of the Kasu River (粕川), a branch of the Ino River, in the southern part of Gunma Prefecture. In the surrounding area, the distribution of Early Kofun period sites and kofun is known, and to the southeast lies the {{ill|Shibazaki Asama-yama Kofun|ja|柴崎浅間山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}. In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|12=draft|12=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}}), grave goods were excavated, and subsequently the mound was leveled. The original type of the tumulus is unclear; {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|12=draft|12=draft}} regarded it as a small {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} of about 12 meters in diameter, while {{ill|Motomura Gōshō|ja|本村豪章|12=draft|12=draft}} considered it a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} with a side length of about 22 meters{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. No thatched stones or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} were observed on the mound’s exterior{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. According to records, the burial facility is estimated to have been a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}; its main axis was oriented north–south, and traces of vermilion were found inside{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. During the excavation in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|12=draft|12=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}}), four bronze mirrors (2 {{ill|triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}) as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} were recovered{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Notably, one of the triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors bears an inscription indicating the first year of the era "□始元年" (presumably the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|12=draft|12=draft}} of the Wei, i.e. {{ill|340|ja|340|12=draft|12=draft}}), making it an important example of the early stage in the development of such mirrors{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The construction period is estimated to be in the latter half of the 4th century of the Early Kofun period{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Despite its small size, this kofun is significant because, in addition to yielding one of the few dated mirrors in Gunma Prefecture, it also contained four bronze mirrors as grave goods. These features—unreflected in the tumulus’s type or map_size—suggest a connection with the Yamato state in the Kinai region, similar only to the {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|ja|長者屋敷天王山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Gunma Prefecture{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|1989|ja|1989|12=draft|12=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Site History == * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|12=draft|12=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}}), grave goods were excavated{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * In {{ill|1926|ja|1926|12=draft|12=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Showa era|ja|昭和|12=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|12=draft|12=draft}} conducted a survey (at which time the mound had been leveled and converted to farmland){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * On June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|12=draft|12=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|12=draft|12=draft}}), the burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990|12=draft|12=draft}} (Heisei 2), a survey was conducted on residential land in the Shimo-Ōruimachi area, but the kofun could not be confirmed (as recorded in the Takasaki City History){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. == burial goods == [[File:蟹沢古墳出土 三角縁同向式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Triangular-edged, same-facing Shikishinju Mirror
(National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}})}}{{small|{{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} exhibition.}}]] The grave goods excavated in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|12=draft|12=draft}} are as follows{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}: * Bronze mirrors: 4 ** {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|12=draft|12=draft}}: 2 ***: One mirror is a triangular-edged, same-facing (stair-step type) Shikishinju mirror, with a diameter of 22.6 cm and a rim thickness of 1.0 cm. It bears the inscription "□始元年 陳是作鏡 自有經迷 本自𦭴師 杜地命出 壽如金石 保子□□" and is presumed to date to the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|340|ja|340|12=draft|12=draft}}) of the Wei, representing an early stage of triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors. Mirrors of similar type have been found at the {{ill|Moriho Kofun|ja|森尾古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Hyogo Prefecture and the {{ill|Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|ja|竹島御家老屋敷古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Yamaguchi Prefecture. ***: Another mirror is a triangular-edged mirror depicting three gods and three beasts, with a diameter of 21.9 cm and a rim thickness of 2.1 cm. Its condition is notably poor. Similar mirrors have been found at the {{ill|Niiyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Nara Prefecture, the {{ill|Manai Kofun|ja|真名井古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Osaka Prefecture, and a site in southern Kyoto (now in the collection of the Kurashiki Archaeological Museum). ** {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}: 2 ***: One mirror is an eight-flower (Hakka) mirror, with a diameter of 10.14 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. ***: Another mirror is a six-flower (Rokka) mirror, with a diameter of 9.8 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. * Iron axes: 2 *: Rectangular iron axes. One piece measures 12.4 cm in length, 4.3 cm in width, and 1.0 cm in thickness; the other measures 8.4 cm in length, 4.4 cm in width, and 1.3 cm in thickness. * Iron adze: 1 *: Some sources refer to it as an iron spear. It is double-edged, with a remaining length of 18.1 cm and a blade width of 1.0 cm. * Fragments of iron swords: 4 fragmentary pieces. * Fragments of a straight sword: with a remaining length of 22.3 cm. * Fragments of {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}: 9 pieces, representing three types – a double-mouthed jar, an S-shaped jar with a rim base, and a jar with high shoulders. == Historic Site == === National Important designation1 (National designation1) === * Gunma Prefecture Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun burial goods (archaeological materials) – details are as follows. These items are held by the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} and were Historic Site1 on June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|12=draft|12=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|12=draft|12=draft}}){{国cultural property等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳Excavated Artifacts}}. ** Bronze mirrors: *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror with an inscription indicating the first year of □始元年. *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror. *** 2 Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors. ** Swords and related items: *** Fragments of iron swords – collectively. *** Fragments of iron sword remnants – collectively. ** 2 pieces of fragmented iron axes. ** 1 piece of fragmented iron halberd. ** 9 pieces of fragmented {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} (located in Taitō, Tokyo) – holds the burial goods from the Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = {{ill|Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} |image = |alternate_name = | part_of = |location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|12=draft|12=draft}} Shibazaki Town (柴崎町) |coordinates = 36 |coordinates = 18 |coordinates = 52.58 |coordinates = 139 |coordinates = 3 |coordinates = 24.43 |omitted = JP-10 |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} |map_size = Diameter 12 m or side length 22 m |burial = (Estimated) {{ill|Clay Coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} |excavations = 2 {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|12=draft|12=draft}}s (including 1 bronze mirror with an inscription from the first year of the Seishi era) and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}s, as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} |Secondary tumulus = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|12=draft|12=draft}} |Interred = |Mausoleum = |Historic Site = None |Tangible designation1 = excavations (National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}) |notes = The mound is no longer extant |map = Japan Gunma |type = |map_dot_label = Shibazaki
Kani-sawa Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun''' (柴崎蟹沢古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Shibazaki Town, {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its type was either a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} or a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound is no longer extant, and the burial goods have been Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == It is a small kofun constructed on a low terrace on the southern bank of the Kasu River (粕川), a branch of the Ino River, in the southern part of Gunma Prefecture. In the surrounding area, the distribution of Early Kofun period sites and kofun is known, and to the southeast lies the {{ill|Shibazaki Asama-yama Kofun|ja|柴崎浅間山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}. In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|12=draft|12=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}}), grave goods were excavated, and subsequently the mound was leveled. The original type of the tumulus is unclear; {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|12=draft|12=draft}} regarded it as a small {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} of about 12 meters in diameter, while {{ill|Motomura Gōshō|ja|本村豪章|12=draft|12=draft}} considered it a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} with a side length of about 22 meters{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. No thatched stones or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} were observed on the mound’s exterior{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. According to records, the burial facility is estimated to have been a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}; its main axis was oriented north–south, and traces of vermilion were found inside{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. During the excavation in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|12=draft|12=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}}), four bronze mirrors (2 {{ill|triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}) as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} were recovered{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Notably, one of the triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors bears an inscription indicating the first year of the era "□始元年" (presumably the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|12=draft|12=draft}} of the Wei, i.e. {{ill|340|ja|340|12=draft|12=draft}}), making it an important example of the early stage in the development of such mirrors{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The construction period is estimated to be in the latter half of the 4th century of the Early Kofun period{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Despite its small size, this kofun is significant because, in addition to yielding one of the few dated mirrors in Gunma Prefecture, it also contained four bronze mirrors as grave goods. These features—unreflected in the tumulus’s type or map_size—suggest a connection with the Yamato state in the Kinai region, similar only to the {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|ja|長者屋敷天王山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Gunma Prefecture{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|1989|ja|1989|12=draft|12=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|12=draft|12=draft}}). == Site History == * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|12=draft|12=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}}), grave goods were excavated{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * In {{ill|1926|ja|1926|12=draft|12=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Showa era|ja|昭和|12=draft|12=draft}}), {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|12=draft|12=draft}} conducted a survey (at which time the mound had been leveled and converted to farmland){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * On June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|12=draft|12=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|12=draft|12=draft}}), the burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990|12=draft|12=draft}} (Heisei 2), a survey was conducted on residential land in the Shimo-Ōruimachi area, but the kofun could not be confirmed (as recorded in the Takasaki City History){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. == burial goods == [[File:蟹沢古墳出土 三角縁同向式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Triangular-edged, same-facing Shikishinju Mirror
(National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}})}}{{small|{{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} exhibition.}}]] The grave goods excavated in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|12=draft|12=draft}} are as follows{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}: * Bronze mirrors: 4 ** {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|12=draft|12=draft}}: 2 ***: One mirror is a triangular-edged, same-facing (stair-step type) Shikishinju mirror, with a diameter of 22.6 cm and a rim thickness of 1.0 cm. It bears the inscription "□始元年 陳是作鏡 自有經迷 本自𦭴師 杜地命出 壽如金石 保子□□" and is presumed to date to the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|340|ja|340|12=draft|12=draft}}) of the Wei, representing an early stage of triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors. Mirrors of similar type have been found at the {{ill|Moriho Kofun|ja|森尾古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Hyogo Prefecture and the {{ill|Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|ja|竹島御家老屋敷古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Yamaguchi Prefecture. ***: Another mirror is a triangular-edged mirror depicting three gods and three beasts, with a diameter of 21.9 cm and a rim thickness of 2.1 cm. Its condition is notably poor. Similar mirrors have been found at the {{ill|Niiyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Nara Prefecture, the {{ill|Manai Kofun|ja|真名井古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} in Osaka Prefecture, and a site in southern Kyoto (now in the collection of the Kurashiki Archaeological Museum). ** {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}: 2 ***: One mirror is an eight-flower (Hakka) mirror, with a diameter of 10.14 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. ***: Another mirror is a six-flower (Rokka) mirror, with a diameter of 9.8 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. * Iron axes: 2 *: Rectangular iron axes. One piece measures 12.4 cm in length, 4.3 cm in width, and 1.0 cm in thickness; the other measures 8.4 cm in length, 4.4 cm in width, and 1.3 cm in thickness. * Iron adze: 1 *: Some sources refer to it as an iron spear. It is double-edged, with a remaining length of 18.1 cm and a blade width of 1.0 cm. * Fragments of iron swords: 4 fragmentary pieces. * Fragments of a straight sword: with a remaining length of 22.3 cm. * Fragments of {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}: 9 pieces, representing three types – a double-mouthed jar, an S-shaped jar with a rim base, and a jar with high shoulders. == Historic Site == === National Important designation1 (National designation1) === * Gunma Prefecture Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun burial goods (archaeological materials) – details are as follows. These items are held by the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} and were Historic Site1 on June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|12=draft|12=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|12=draft|12=draft}}){{国cultural property等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳Excavated Artifacts}}. ** Bronze mirrors: *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror with an inscription indicating the first year of □始元年. *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror. *** 2 Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors. ** Swords and related items: *** Fragments of iron swords – collectively. *** Fragments of iron sword remnants – collectively. ** 2 pieces of fragmented iron axes. ** 1 piece of fragmented iron halberd. ** 9 pieces of fragmented {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} (located in Taitō, Tokyo) – holds the burial goods from the Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|lt=Nihon Kofun Daijiten|12=draft|12=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|蟹沢古墳（古墳）|1989}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=|year=1999|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title=New Edited Takasaki City History|volume=Materials Edition 1: Early Ancient I|publisher={{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|12=draft|12=draft}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Bronze But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = {{ill|Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |image = |alternate_name = | part_of = |location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Shibazaki Town (柴崎町) |coordinates = 36 |coordinates = 18 |coordinates = 52.58 |coordinates = 139 |coordinates = 3 |coordinates = 24.43 |omitted = JP-10 |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |map_size = Diameter 12 m or side length 22 m |burial = (Estimated) {{ill|Clay Coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |excavations = 2 {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}s (including 1 bronze mirror with an inscription from the first year of the Seishi era) and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}s, as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Secondary tumulus = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Interred = |Mausoleum = |Historic Site = None |Tangible designation1 = excavations (National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |notes = The mound is no longer extant |map = Japan Gunma |type = |map_dot_label = Shibazaki
Kani-sawa Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun''' (柴崎蟹沢古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Shibazaki Town, {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its type was either a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The mound is no longer extant, and the burial goods have been Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == It is a small kofun constructed on a low terrace on the southern bank of the Kasu River (粕川), a branch of the Ino River, in the southern part of Gunma Prefecture. In the surrounding area, the distribution of Early Kofun period sites and kofun is known, and to the southeast lies the {{ill|Shibazaki Asama-yama Kofun|ja|柴崎浅間山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), grave goods were excavated, and subsequently the mound was leveled. The original type of the tumulus is unclear; {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|qq=draft}} regarded it as a small {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of about 12 meters in diameter, while {{ill|Motomura Gōshō|ja|本村豪章|qq=draft|qq=draft}} considered it a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with a side length of about 22 meters{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. No thatched stones or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were observed on the mound’s exterior{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. According to records, the burial facility is estimated to have been a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}; its main axis was oriented north–south, and traces of vermilion were found inside{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. During the excavation in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), four bronze mirrors (2 {{ill|triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were recovered{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Notably, one of the triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors bears an inscription indicating the first year of the era "□始元年" (presumably the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the Wei, i.e. {{ill|340|ja|340|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), making it an important example of the early stage in the development of such mirrors{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The construction period is estimated to be in the latter half of the 4th century of the Early Kofun period{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Despite its small size, this kofun is significant because, in addition to yielding one of the few dated mirrors in Gunma Prefecture, it also contained four bronze mirrors as grave goods. These features—unreflected in the tumulus’s type or map_size—suggest a connection with the Yamato state in the Kinai region, similar only to the {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|ja|長者屋敷天王山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Gunma Prefecture{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Site History == * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), grave goods were excavated{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * In {{ill|1926|ja|1926|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Showa era|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|qq=draft}} conducted a survey (at which time the mound had been leveled and converted to farmland){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * On June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Heisei 2), a survey was conducted on residential land in the Shimo-Ōruimachi area, but the kofun could not be confirmed (as recorded in the Takasaki City History){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. == burial goods == [[File:蟹沢古墳出土 三角縁同向式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Triangular-edged, same-facing Shikishinju Mirror
(National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}})}}{{small|{{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} exhibition.}}]] The grave goods excavated in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are as follows{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}: * Bronze mirrors: 4 ** {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 2 ***: One mirror is a triangular-edged, same-facing (stair-step type) Shikishinju mirror, with a diameter of 22.6 cm and a rim thickness of 1.0 cm. It bears the inscription "□始元年 陳是作鏡 自有經迷 本自𦭴師 杜地命出 壽如金石 保子□□" and is presumed to date to the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|340|ja|340|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) of the Wei, representing an early stage of triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors. Mirrors of similar type have been found at the {{ill|Moriho Kofun|ja|森尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Hyogo Prefecture and the {{ill|Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|ja|竹島御家老屋敷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Yamaguchi Prefecture. ***: Another mirror is a triangular-edged mirror depicting three gods and three beasts, with a diameter of 21.9 cm and a rim thickness of 2.1 cm. Its condition is notably poor. Similar mirrors have been found at the {{ill|Niiyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Nara Prefecture, the {{ill|Manai Kofun|ja|真名井古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Osaka Prefecture, and a site in southern Kyoto (now in the collection of the Kurashiki Archaeological Museum). ** {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 2 ***: One mirror is an eight-flower (Hakka) mirror, with a diameter of 10.14 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. ***: Another mirror is a six-flower (Rokka) mirror, with a diameter of 9.8 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. * Iron axes: 2 *: Rectangular iron axes. One piece measures 12.4 cm in length, 4.3 cm in width, and 1.0 cm in thickness; the other measures 8.4 cm in length, 4.4 cm in width, and 1.3 cm in thickness. * Iron adze: 1 *: Some sources refer to it as an iron spear. It is double-edged, with a remaining length of 18.1 cm and a blade width of 1.0 cm. * Fragments of iron swords: 4 fragmentary pieces. * Fragments of a straight sword: with a remaining length of 22.3 cm. * Fragments of {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 9 pieces, representing three types – a double-mouthed jar, an S-shaped jar with a rim base, and a jar with high shoulders. == Historic Site == === National Important designation1 (National designation1) === * Gunma Prefecture Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun burial goods (archaeological materials) – details are as follows. These items are held by the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and were Historic Site1 on June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}){{国cultural property等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳Excavated Artifacts}}. ** Bronze mirrors: *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror with an inscription indicating the first year of □始元年. *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror. *** 2 Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors. ** Swords and related items: *** Fragments of iron swords – collectively. *** Fragments of iron sword remnants – collectively. ** 2 pieces of fragmented iron axes. ** 1 piece of fragmented iron halberd. ** 9 pieces of fragmented {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (located in Taitō, Tokyo) – holds the burial goods from the Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Saburō Kobayashi (archaeologist)|ja|小林三郎 (考古学者)|lt=Saburō Kobayashi|lt_ja=小林三郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|year=1989|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|lt=Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|蟹沢古墳（古墳）|1989}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=|year=1999|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title=New Edited Takasaki City History|volume=Materials Edition 1: Early Ancient I|publisher={{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Bronze mirror#Inscriptions|ja|銅鏡#紀年銘鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * {{国cultural property等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳Excavated Artifacts}} {{citation needed|しはさきかにさわこふん}} of Tokyo]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages t]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:ted]] [[Category:Ancient Tombs in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takasaki City]] [[Category:National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft}} [[Category:translated pages]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with revision.comment: 4 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:柴崎蟹沢古墳 up to rev 101952536
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 柴崎蟹沢古墳 |画像 = |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[高崎市]]柴崎町 |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 18|緯度秒 = 52.58 |経度度 = 139|経度分 = 3|経度秒 = 24.43 |ISO = JP-10 |形状 = [[円墳]]または[[方墳]] |規模 = 直径12mまたは一辺22m |埋葬施設 = （推定）[[粘土槨]] |出土品 = [[三角縁神獣鏡]]2面（正始元年紀年銘鏡1面）・[[内行花文鏡]]2面ほか鉄斧・鉄鑿・鉄剣・直刀・[[土師器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[4世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = 出土品（国の[[重要文化財]]） |特記事項 = 墳丘は非現存 |地図 = Japan Gunma |アイコン = |ラベル = 柴崎
蟹沢古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} '''柴崎蟹沢古墳'''（しばざきかにさわこふん）は、[[群馬県]][[高崎市]]柴崎町にあった[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]または[[方墳]]。現在では墳丘は失われている。出土品は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 == 概要 == 群馬県南部、井野川支流の粕川南岸の低段丘上に築造された小古墳である。一帯では古墳時代前期の遺跡・古墳の分布が知られ、南東には柴崎浅間山古墳が所在する。[[1909年]]（[[明治]]42年）に副葬品が出土し、その後に墳丘は削平されている。 元の墳形は明らかでないが、[[森本六爾]]は直径12メートル程度の小[[円墳]]とし、本村豪章は一辺22メートル程度の[[方墳]]とする{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}。墳丘外表で[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]は認められなかったという{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}。埋葬施設は、記録によれば[[粘土槨]]と推定される{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}。粘土槨の主軸は南北方向で、内部では朱の痕跡があったという{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}。明治42年の発掘では、副葬品として銅鏡4面（[[三角縁神獣鏡]]2面・[[内行花文鏡]]2面）のほか鉄斧・鉄鑿・鉄剣・直刀・[[土師器]]が出土している{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}。特に三角縁神獣鏡のうち1面は、「□始元年」の紀年銘（[[魏 (三国)|魏]]の元号の[[正始 (魏)|正始]]元年（[[340年]]）か）を持つとともに、三角縁神獣鏡の初期段階のものとしても注目される{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]前期の[[4世紀]]後半頃と推定される{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}。小古墳でありながら、数少ない紀年銘鏡を出土したことに加えて、銅鏡4面も副葬する前期古墳は群馬県内では[[前橋天神山古墳]]・[[長者屋敷天王山古墳]]のみである点で注目され、墳形・規模には反映されない別の次元での畿内[[ヤマト王権]]との関係を示唆するとして重要視される古墳になる{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}。 出土品は[[1989年]]（[[平成]]元年）に国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1909年]]（[[明治]]42年）、副葬品の出土{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}。 * [[1926年]]（[[昭和]]元年）、[[森本六爾]]による踏査（当時には削平・畑地化）{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}。 * [[1989年]]（[[平成]]元年）6月12日、出土品が国の[[重要文化財]]に指定。 * [[1990年]]（平成2年）、下大類町内の宅地における確認調査。古墳の確認には至らず（高崎市市史編さん室）{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}。 == 出土品 == [[ファイル:蟹沢古墳出土 三角縁同向式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|三角縁同向式神獣鏡
（国の[[重要文化財]]）}}{{small|[[東京国立博物館]]展示。}}]] 明治42年の発掘で出土した副葬品は次の通り{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}。 * 銅鏡 4 ** [[三角縁神獣鏡]] 2 **: 1面は三角縁同向式（階段式）神獣鏡で、直径22.6センチメートル・縁厚1.0センチメートル。「□始元年 陳是作鏡 自有經迷 本自𦭴師 杜地命出 壽如金石 保子□□」の銘文を有し、紀年銘は[[魏 (三国)|魏]]の元号の[[正始 (魏)|正始]]元年（[[340年]]）と推測される。同型鏡は森尾古墳出土鏡（兵庫県）・竹島御家老屋敷古墳出土鏡（山口県）。 **: 別の1面は三角縁三神三獣鏡で、直径21.9センチメートル・縁厚2.1センチメートル。遺存状況は著しく悪い。同型鏡は[[新山古墳]]出土鏡（奈良県）・真名井古墳出土鏡（大阪府）・伝京都南部出土鏡（倉敷考古館所蔵）。 ** [[内行花文鏡]] 2 **: 1面は八花文鏡で、直径10.14センチメートル・縁厚0.3センチメートル。 **: 別の1面は六花文鏡で、直径9.8センチメートル・縁厚0.3センチメートル。 * 鉄斧 2 *: 短冊状鉄斧。1点は長さ12.4センチメートル・幅4.3センチメートル・厚さ1.0センチメートル、もう1点は長さ8.4センチメートル・幅4.4センチメートル・厚さ1.3センチメートル。 * 鉄鑿 1 *: 文献によっては鉄槍とする。両刃、残存長18.1センチメートル・刃部幅1.0センチメートル。 * 鉄剣残片 *: 破片状4点。 * 直刀残片 *: 残存長22.3センチメートル。 * [[土師器]]片 9 *: 二重口縁壺、S字状口縁台付甕、高坏の3種類。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 群馬県蟹沢古墳出土品（考古資料） - 明細は以下。東京国立博物館保管。1989年（平成元年）6月12日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳出土品}}。 ** 銅鏡 *** 三角縁神獣鏡 1面 - □始元年の銘がある。 *** 三角縁神獣鏡 1面 *** 内行花文鏡 2面 ** 刀剣類 *** 鉄刀残欠 一括 *** 鉄剣残欠 一括 ** 鉄斧残欠 2箇 ** 鉄鉇残欠 1本 ** 土師器残欠 9箇 == 関連施設 == * [[東京国立博物館]]（東京都台東区） - 柴崎蟹沢古墳の出土品を保管。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[小林三郎 (考古学者)|小林三郎]]|year=1989|chapter=蟹沢古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|蟹沢古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=|year=1999|chapter=柴崎蟹沢古墳|title=新編高崎市史|volume=資料編1 原始古代I|publisher=高崎市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[銅鏡#紀年銘鏡]] == 外部リンク == * {{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳出土品}} {{デフォルトソート:しはさきかにさわこふん}} [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:高崎市の歴史]] [[Category:東京都の重要文化財]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[柴崎蟹沢古墳]] to [[Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
