{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = {{ill|Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |image = |alternate_name = | part_of = |location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Shibazaki Town (柴崎町) |coordinates = 36 |coordinates = 18 |coordinates = 52.58 |coordinates = 139 |coordinates = 3 |coordinates = 24.43 |omitted = JP-10 |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |map_size = Diameter 12 m or side length 22 m |burial = (Estimated) {{ill|Clay Coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |excavations = 2 {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}s (including 1 bronze mirror with an inscription from the first year of the Seishi era) and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}s, as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Secondary tumulus = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Interred = |Mausoleum = |Historic Site = None |Tangible designation1 = excavations (National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |notes = The mound is no longer extant |map = Japan Gunma |type = |map_dot_label = Shibazaki
Kani-sawa Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun''' (柴崎蟹沢古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Shibazaki Town, {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its type was either a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The mound is no longer extant, and the burial goods have been Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == It is a small kofun constructed on a low terrace on the southern bank of the Kasu River (粕川), a branch of the Ino River, in the southern part of Gunma Prefecture. In the surrounding area, the distribution of Early Kofun period sites and kofun is known, and to the southeast lies the {{ill|Shibazaki Asama-yama Kofun|ja|柴崎浅間山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), grave goods were excavated, and subsequently the mound was leveled. The original type of the tumulus is unclear; {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|qq=draft}} regarded it as a small {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of about 12 meters in diameter, while {{ill|Motomura Gōshō|ja|本村豪章|qq=draft|qq=draft}} considered it a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with a side length of about 22 meters{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. No thatched stones or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were observed on the mound’s exterior{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. According to records, the burial facility is estimated to have been a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}; its main axis was oriented north–south, and traces of vermilion were found inside{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. During the excavation in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), four bronze mirrors (2 {{ill|triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were recovered{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Notably, one of the triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors bears an inscription indicating the first year of the era "□始元年" (presumably the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the Wei, i.e. {{ill|340|ja|340|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), making it an important example of the early stage in the development of such mirrors{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The construction period is estimated to be in the latter half of the 4th century of the Early Kofun period{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Despite its small size, this kofun is significant because, in addition to yielding one of the few dated mirrors in Gunma Prefecture, it also contained four bronze mirrors as grave goods. These features—unreflected in the tumulus’s type or map_size—suggest a connection with the Yamato state in the Kinai region, similar only to the {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|ja|長者屋敷天王山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Gunma Prefecture{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Site History == * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), grave goods were excavated{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * In {{ill|1926|ja|1926|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Showa era|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|qq=draft}} conducted a survey (at which time the mound had been leveled and converted to farmland){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * On June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Heisei 2), a survey was conducted on residential land in the Shimo-Ōruimachi area, but the kofun could not be confirmed (as recorded in the Takasaki City History){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. == burial goods == [[File:蟹沢古墳出土 三角縁同向式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Triangular-edged, same-facing Shikishinju Mirror
(National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}})}}{{small|{{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} exhibition.}}]] The grave goods excavated in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are as follows{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}: * Bronze mirrors: 4 ** {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 2 ***: One mirror is a triangular-edged, same-facing (stair-step type) Shikishinju mirror, with a diameter of 22.6 cm and a rim thickness of 1.0 cm. It bears the inscription "□始元年 陳是作鏡 自有經迷 本自𦭴師 杜地命出 壽如金石 保子□□" and is presumed to date to the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|340|ja|340|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) of the Wei, representing an early stage of triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors. Mirrors of similar type have been found at the {{ill|Moriho Kofun|ja|森尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Hyogo Prefecture and the {{ill|Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|ja|竹島御家老屋敷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Yamaguchi Prefecture. ***: Another mirror is a triangular-edged mirror depicting three gods and three beasts, with a diameter of 21.9 cm and a rim thickness of 2.1 cm. Its condition is notably poor. Similar mirrors have been found at the {{ill|Niiyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Nara Prefecture, the {{ill|Manai Kofun|ja|真名井古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Osaka Prefecture, and a site in southern Kyoto (now in the collection of the Kurashiki Archaeological Museum). ** {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 2 ***: One mirror is an eight-flower (Hakka) mirror, with a diameter of 10.14 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. ***: Another mirror is a six-flower (Rokka) mirror, with a diameter of 9.8 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. * Iron axes: 2 *: Rectangular iron axes. One piece measures 12.4 cm in length, 4.3 cm in width, and 1.0 cm in thickness; the other measures 8.4 cm in length, 4.4 cm in width, and 1.3 cm in thickness. * Iron adze: 1 *: Some sources refer to it as an iron spear. It is double-edged, with a remaining length of 18.1 cm and a blade width of 1.0 cm. * Fragments of iron swords: 4 fragmentary pieces. * Fragments of a straight sword: with a remaining length of 22.3 cm. * Fragments of {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 9 pieces, representing three types – a double-mouthed jar, an S-shaped jar with a rim base, and a jar with high shoulders. == Historic Site == === National Important designation1 (National designation1) === * Gunma Prefecture Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun burial goods (archaeological materials) – details are as follows. These items are held by the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and were Historic Site1 on June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}){{国cultural property等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳Excavated Artifacts}}. ** Bronze mirrors: *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror with an inscription indicating the first year of □始元年. *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror. *** 2 Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors. ** Swords and related items: *** Fragments of iron swords – collectively. *** Fragments of iron sword remnants – collectively. ** 2 pieces of fragmented iron axes. ** 1 piece of fragmented iron halberd. ** 9 pieces of fragmented {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (located in Taitō, Tokyo) – holds the burial goods from the Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|lt=Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|蟹沢古墳（古墳）|1989}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=|year=1999|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title=New Edited Takasaki City Despite its small size, this kofun is significant because, in addition to yielding one of the few dated mirrors in Gunma Prefecture, it also contained four bronze mirrors as grave goods. These features—unreflected in the tumulus’s type or map_size—suggest a connection with the Yamato state in the Kinai region, similar only to the {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|ja|長者屋敷天王山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Gunma Prefecture{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Site History == * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), grave goods were excavated{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * In {{ill|1926|ja|1926|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Showa era|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|qq=draft}} conducted a survey (at which time the mound had been leveled and converted to farmland){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * On June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Heisei 2), a survey was conducted on residential land in the Shimo-Ōruimachi area, but the kofun could not be confirmed (as recorded in the Takasaki City History){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. == burial goods == [[File:蟹沢古墳出土 三角縁同向式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Triangular-edged, same-facing Shikishinju Mirror
(National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}})}}{{small|{{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} exhibition.}}]] The grave goods excavated in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are as follows{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}: * Bronze mirrors: 4 ** {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 2 ***: One mirror is a triangular-edged, same-facing (stair-step type) Shikishinju mirror, with a diameter of 22.6 cm and a rim thickness of 1.0 cm. It bears the inscription "□始元年 陳是作鏡 自有經迷 本自𦭴師 杜地命出 壽如金石 保子□□" and is presumed to date to the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|340|ja|340|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) of the Wei, representing an early stage of triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors. Mirrors of similar type have been found at the {{ill|Moriho Kofun|ja|森尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Hyogo Prefecture and the {{ill|Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|ja|竹島御家老屋敷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Yamaguchi Prefecture. ***: Another mirror is a triangular-edged mirror depicting three gods and three beasts, with a diameter of 21.9 cm and a rim thickness of 2.1 cm. Its condition is notably poor. Similar mirrors have been found at the {{ill|Niiyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Nara Prefecture, the {{ill|Manai Kofun|ja|真名井古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Osaka Prefecture, and a site in southern Kyoto (now in the collection of the Kurashiki Archaeological Museum). ** {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 2 ***: One mirror is an eight-flower (Hakka) mirror, with a diameter of 10.14 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. ***: Another mirror is a six-flower (Rokka) mirror, with a diameter of 9.8 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. * Iron axes: 2 *: Rectangular iron axes. One piece measures 12.4 cm in length, 4.3 cm in width, and 1.0 cm in thickness; the other measures 8.4 cm in length, 4.4 cm in width, and 1.3 cm in thickness. * Iron adze: 1 *: Some sources refer to it as an iron spear. It is double-edged, with a remaining length of 18.1 cm and a blade width of 1.0 cm. * Fragments of iron swords: 4 fragmentary pieces. * Fragments of a straight sword: with a remaining length of 22.3 cm. * Fragments of {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 9 pieces, representing three types – a double-mouthed jar, an S-shaped jar with a rim base, and a jar with high shoulders. == Historic Site == === National Important designation1 (National designation1) === * Gunma Prefecture Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun burial goods (archaeological materials) – details are as follows. These items are held by the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and were Historic Site1 on June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}){{国cultural property等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳Excavated Artifacts}}. ** Bronze mirrors: *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror with an inscription indicating the first year of □始元年. *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror. *** 2 Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors. ** Swords and related items: *** Fragments of iron swords – collectively. *** Fragments of iron sword remnants – collectively. ** 2 pieces of fragmented iron axes. ** 1 piece of fragmented iron halberd. ** 9 pieces of fragmented {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (located in Taitō, Tokyo) – holds the burial goods from the Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kani-sawa Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun''' (柴崎蟹沢古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Shibazaki Town, {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its type was either a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The mound is no longer extant, and the burial goods have been Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == It is a small kofun constructed on a low terrace on the southern bank of the Kasu River (粕川), a branch of the Ino River, in the southern part of Gunma Prefecture. In the surrounding area, the distribution of Early Kofun period sites and kofun is known, and to the southeast lies the {{ill|Shibazaki Asama-yama Kofun|ja|柴崎浅間山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), grave goods were excavated, and subsequently the mound was leveled. The original type of the tumulus is unclear; {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|qq=draft}} regarded it as a small {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of about 12 meters in diameter, while {{ill|Motomura Gōshō|ja|本村豪章|qq=draft|qq=draft}} considered it a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with a side length of about 22 meters{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. No thatched stones or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were observed on the mound’s exterior{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. According to records, the burial facility is estimated to have been a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}; its main axis was oriented north–south, and traces of vermilion were found inside{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. During the excavation in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), four bronze mirrors (2 {{ill|triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were recovered{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Notably, one of the triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors bears an inscription indicating the first year of the era "□始元年" (presumably the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the Wei, i.e. {{ill|340|ja|340|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), making it an important example of the early stage in the development of such mirrors{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The construction period is estimated to be in the latter half of the 4th century of the Early Kofun period{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Despite its small size, this kofun is significant because, in addition to yielding one of the few dated mirrors in Gunma Prefecture, it also contained four bronze mirrors as grave goods. These features—unreflected in the tumulus’s type or map_size—suggest a connection with the Yamato state in the Kinai region, similar only to the {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|ja|長者屋敷天王山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Gunma Prefecture{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Site History == * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), grave goods were excavated{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * In {{ill|1926|ja|1926|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Showa era|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|qq=draft}} conducted a survey (at which time the mound had been leveled and converted to farmland){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * On June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Heisei 2), a survey was conducted on residential land in the Shimo-Ōruimachi area, but the kofun could not be confirmed (as recorded in the Takasaki City History){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. == burial goods == [[File:蟹沢古墳出土 三角縁同向式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Triangular-edged, same-facing Shikishinju Mirror
(National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}})}}{{small|{{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} exhibition.}}]] The grave goods excavated in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are as follows{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}: * Bronze mirrors: 4 ** {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 2 ***: One mirror is a triangular-edged, same-facing (stair-step type) Shikishinju mirror, with a diameter of 22.6 cm and a rim thickness of 1.0 cm. It bears the inscription "□始元年 陳是作鏡 自有經迷 本自𦭴師 杜地命出 壽如金石 保子□□" and is presumed to date to the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|340|ja|340|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) of the Wei, representing an early stage of triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors. Mirrors of similar type have been found at the {{ill|Moriho Kofun|ja|森尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Hyogo Prefecture and the {{ill|Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|ja|竹島御家老屋敷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Yamaguchi Prefecture. ***: Another mirror is a triangular-edged mirror depicting three gods and three beasts, with a diameter of 21.9 cm and a rim thickness of 2.1 cm. Its condition is notably poor. Similar mirrors have been found at the {{ill|Niiyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Nara Prefecture, the {{ill|Manai Kofun|ja|真名井古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Osaka Prefecture, and a site in southern Kyoto (now in the collection of the Kurashiki Archaeological Museum). ** {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 2 ***: One mirror is an eight-flower (Hakka) mirror, with a diameter of 10.14 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. ***: Another mirror is a six-flower (Rokka) mirror, with a diameter of 9.8 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. * Iron axes: 2 *: Rectangular iron axes. One piece measures 12.4 cm in length, 4.3 cm in width, and 1.0 cm in thickness; the other measures 8.4 cm in length, 4.4 cm in width, and 1.3 cm in thickness. * Iron adze: 1 *: Some sources refer to it as an iron spear. It is double-edged, with a remaining length of 18.1 cm and a blade width of 1.0 cm. * Fragments of iron swords: 4 fragmentary pieces. * Fragments of a straight sword: with a remaining length of 22.3 cm. * Fragments of {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 9 pieces, representing three types – a double-mouthed jar, an S-shaped jar with a rim base, and a jar with high shoulders. == Historic Site == === National Important designation1 (National designation1) === * Gunma Prefecture Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun burial goods (archaeological materials) – details are as follows. era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}){{国cultural property等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳Excavated Artifacts}}. ** Bronze mirrors: *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror with an inscription indicating the first year of □始元年. *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror. *** 2 Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors. ** Swords and related items: *** Fragments of iron swords – collectively. *** Fragments of iron sword remnants – Kani-sawa Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun''' (柴崎蟹沢古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Shibazaki Town, {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its type was either a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The mound is no longer extant, and the burial goods have been Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == It is a small kofun constructed on a low terrace on the southern bank of the Kasu River (粕川), a branch of the Ino River, in the southern part of Gunma Prefecture. In the surrounding area, the distribution of Early Kofun period sites and kofun is known, and to the southeast lies the {{ill|Shibazaki Asama-yama Kofun|ja|柴崎浅間山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), grave goods were excavated, and subsequently the mound was leveled. The original type of the tumulus is unclear; {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|qq=draft}} regarded it as a small {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of about 12 meters in diameter, while {{ill|Motomura Gōshō|ja|本村豪章|qq=draft|qq=draft}} considered it a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with a side length of about 22 meters{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. No thatched stones or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were observed on the mound’s exterior{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. According to records, the burial facility is estimated to have been a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}; its main axis was oriented north–south, and traces of vermilion were found inside{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. During the excavation in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), four bronze mirrors (2 {{ill|triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were recovered{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Notably, one of the triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors bears an inscription indicating the first year of the era "□始元年" (presumably the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the Wei, i.e. {{ill|340|ja|340|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), making it an important example of the early stage in the development of such mirrors{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The construction period is estimated to be in the latter half of the 4th century of the Early Kofun period{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Despite its small size, this kofun is significant because, in addition to yielding one of the few dated mirrors in Gunma Prefecture, it also contained four bronze mirrors as grave goods. These features—unreflected in the tumulus’s type or map_size—suggest a connection with the Yamato state in the Kinai region, similar only to the {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|ja|長者屋敷天王山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Gunma Prefecture{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Site History == * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), grave goods were excavated{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * In {{ill|1926|ja|1926|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Showa era|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|qq=draft}} conducted a survey (at which time the mound had been leveled and converted to farmland){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * On June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Heisei 2), a survey was conducted on residential land in the Shimo-Ōruimachi area, but the kofun could not be confirmed (as recorded in the Takasaki City History){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. == burial goods == [[File:蟹沢古墳出土 三角縁同向式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Triangular-edged, same-facing Shikishinju Mirror
(National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}})}}{{small|{{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} exhibition.}}]] The grave goods excavated in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are as follows{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}: * Bronze mirrors: 4 ** {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 2 ***: One mirror is a triangular-edged, same-facing (stair-step type) Shikishinju mirror, with a diameter of 22.6 cm and a rim thickness of 1.0 cm. It bears the inscription "□始元年 陳是作鏡 自有經迷 本自𦭴師 杜地命出 壽如金石 保子□□" and is presumed to date to the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|340|ja|340|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) of the Wei, representing an early stage of triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors. Mirrors of similar type have been found at the {{ill|Moriho Kofun|ja|森尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Hyogo Prefecture and the {{ill|Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|ja|竹島御家老屋敷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Yamaguchi Prefecture. ***: Another mirror is a triangular-edged mirror depicting three gods and three beasts, with a diameter of 21.9 cm and a rim thickness of 2.1 cm. Its condition is notably poor. Similar mirrors have been found at the {{ill|Niiyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Nara Prefecture, the {{ill|Manai Kofun|ja|真名井古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Osaka Prefecture, and a site in southern Kyoto (now in the collection of the Kurashiki Archaeological Museum). ** {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 2 ***: One mirror is an eight-flower (Hakka) mirror, with a diameter of 10.14 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. ***: Another mirror is a six-flower (Rokka) mirror, with a diameter of 9.8 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. * Iron axes: 2 *: Rectangular iron axes. One piece measures 12.4 cm in length, 4.3 cm in width, and 1.0 cm in thickness; the other measures 8.4 cm in length, 4.4 cm in width, and 1.3 cm in thickness. * Iron adze: 1 *: Some sources refer to it as an iron spear. It is double-edged, with a remaining length of 18.1 cm and a blade width of 1.0 cm. * Fragments of iron swords: 4 fragmentary pieces. * Fragments of a straight sword: with a remaining length of 22.3 cm. * Fragments of {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 9 pieces, representing three types – a double-mouthed jar, an S-shaped jar with a rim base, and a jar with high shoulders. == Historic Site == === National Important designation1 (National designation1) === * Gunma Prefecture Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun burial goods (archaeological materials) – details are as follows. These items are held by the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and were Historic Site1 on June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}){{国cultural property等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳Excavated Artifacts}}. ** Bronze mirrors: *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror with an inscription indicating the first year of □始元年. *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror. *** 2 Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors. ** Swords and related items: *** Fragments of iron swords – collectively. *** Fragments of iron sword remnants – collectively. ** 2 pieces of fragmented iron axes. ** 1 piece of fragmented iron halberd. ** 9 pieces of fragmented {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (located in Taitō, Tokyo) – holds the burial goods from the Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|lt=Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|蟹沢古墳（古墳）|1989}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=|year=1999|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title=New Edited Takasaki City History|volume=Materials Edition 1: Early Ancient I|publisher={{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Bronze mirror#Inscriptions|ja|銅鏡#紀年銘鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * {{国cultural property等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳Excavated Artifacts}} {{citation needed|しはさきかにさわこふん}} of Tokyo]] }} According to records, the burial facility is estimated to have been a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}; its main axis was oriented north–south, and traces of vermilion were found inside{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. During the excavation in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), four bronze mirrors (2 {{ill|triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were recovered{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Notably, one of the triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors bears an inscription indicating the first year of the era "□始元年" (presumably the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the Wei, i.e. {{ill|340|ja|340|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), making it an important example of the early stage in the development of such mirrors{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The construction period is estimated to be in the latter half of the 4th century of the Early Kofun period{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Despite its small size, this kofun is significant because, in addition to yielding one of the few dated mirrors in Gunma Prefecture, it also contained four bronze mirrors as grave goods. These features—unreflected in the tumulus’s type or map_size—suggest a connection with the Yamato state in the Kinai region, similar only to the {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|ja|長者屋敷天王山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Gunma Prefecture{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Site History == * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), grave goods were excavated{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * In {{ill|1926|ja|1926|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Showa era|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|qq=draft}} conducted a survey (at which time the mound had been leveled and converted to farmland){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * On June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Heisei 2), a survey was conducted on residential land in the Shimo-Ōruimachi area, but the kofun could not be confirmed (as recorded in the Takasaki City History){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. == burial goods == [[File:蟹沢古墳出土 三角縁同向式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Triangular-edged, same-facing Shikishinju Mirror
(National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}})}}{{small|{{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} exhibition.}}]] The grave goods excavated in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are as follows{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}: * Bronze mirrors: 4 ** {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 2 ***: One mirror is a triangular-edged, same-facing (stair-step type) Shikishinju mirror, with a diameter of 22.6 cm and a rim thickness of 1.0 cm. It bears the inscription "□始元年 陳是作鏡 自有經迷 本自𦭴師 杜地命出 壽如金石 保子□□" and is presumed to date to the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|340|ja|340|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) of the Wei, representing an early stage of triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors. Mirrors of similar type have been found at the {{ill|Moriho Kofun|ja|森尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Hyogo Prefecture and the {{ill|Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|ja|竹島御家老屋敷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Yamaguchi Prefecture. ***: Another mirror is a triangular-edged mirror depicting three gods and three beasts, with a diameter of 21.9 cm and a rim thickness of 2.1 cm. Its condition is notably poor. Similar mirrors have been found at the {{ill|Niiyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Nara Prefecture, the {{ill|Manai Kofun|ja|真名井古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Osaka Prefecture, and a site in southern Kyoto (now in the collection of the Kurashiki Archaeological Museum). ** {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 2 ***: One mirror is an eight-flower (Hakka) mirror, with a diameter of 10.14 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. ***: Another mirror is a six-flower (Rokka) mirror, with a diameter of 9.8 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. * Iron axes: 2 *: Rectangular iron axes. One piece measures 12.4 cm in length, 4.3 cm in width, and 1.0 cm in thickness; the other measures 8.4 cm in length, 4.4 cm in width, and 1.3 cm in thickness. * Iron adze: 1 *: Some sources refer to it as an iron spear. It is double-edged, with a remaining length of 18.1 cm and a blade width of 1.0 cm. * Fragments of iron swords: 4 fragmentary pieces. * Fragments of a straight sword: with a remaining length of 22.3 cm. * Fragments of {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 9 pieces, representing three types – a double-mouthed jar, an S-shaped jar with a rim base, and a jar with high shoulders. == Historic Site == === National Important designation1 (National designation1) === * Gunma Prefecture Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun burial goods (archaeological materials) – details are as follows. These items are held by the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and were Historic Site1 on June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}){{国cultural property等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳Excavated Artifacts}}. ** Bronze mirrors: *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror with an inscription indicating the first year of □始元年. *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror. *** 2 Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors. ** Swords and related items: *** Fragments of iron swords – collectively. *** Fragments of iron sword remnants – collectively. ** 2 pieces of fragmented iron axes. ** 1 piece of fragmented iron halberd. ** 9 pieces of fragmented {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (located in Taitō, Tokyo) – holds the burial goods from the Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kani-sawa Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun''' (柴崎蟹沢古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Shibazaki Town, {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its type was either a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The mound is no longer extant, and the burial goods have been Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == It is a small kofun constructed on a low terrace on the southern bank of the Kasu River (粕川), a branch of the Ino River, in the southern part of Gunma Prefecture. In the surrounding area, the distribution of Early Kofun period sites and kofun is known, and to the southeast lies the {{ill|Shibazaki Asama-yama Kofun|ja|柴崎浅間山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), grave goods were excavated, and subsequently the mound was leveled. The original type of the tumulus is unclear; {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|qq=draft}} regarded it as a small {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of about 12 meters in diameter, while {{ill|Motomura Gōshō|ja|本村豪章|qq=draft|qq=draft}} considered it a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with a side length of about 22 meters{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. No thatched stones or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were observed on the mound’s exterior{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. According to records, the burial facility is estimated to have been a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}; its main axis was oriented north–south, and traces of vermilion were found inside{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. During the excavation in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), four bronze mirrors (2 {{ill|triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were recovered{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Notably, one of the triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors bears an inscription indicating the first year of the era "□始元年" (presumably the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the Wei, i.e. {{ill|340|ja|340|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), making it an important example of the early stage in the development of such mirrors{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The construction period is estimated to be in the latter half of the 4th century of the Early Kofun period{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Despite its small size, this kofun is significant because, in addition to yielding one of the few dated mirrors in Gunma Prefecture, it also contained four bronze mirrors as grave goods. These features—unreflected in the tumulus’s type or map_size—suggest a connection with the Yamato state in the Kinai region, similar only to the {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|ja|長者屋敷天王山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Gunma Prefecture{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Site History == * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), grave goods were excavated{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * In {{ill|1926|ja|1926|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Showa era|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|qq=draft}} conducted a survey (at which time the mound had been leveled and converted to farmland){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * On June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Heisei 2), a survey was conducted on residential land in the Shimo-Ōruimachi area, but the kofun could not be confirmed (as recorded in the Takasaki City History){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. == burial goods == [[File:蟹沢古墳出土 三角縁同向式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Triangular-edged, same-facing Shikishinju Mirror

It bears the inscription "□始元年 陳是作鏡 自有經迷 本自𦭴師 杜地命出 壽如金石 保子□□" and is presumed to date to the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|340|ja|340|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) of the Wei, representing an early stage of triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors. Mirrors of similar type have been found at the {{ill|Moriho Kofun|ja|森尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Hyogo Prefecture and the {{ill|Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|ja|竹島御家老屋敷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Yamaguchi Prefecture. ***: Another mirror is a triangular-edged mirror depicting three gods and three beasts, with a diameter of 21.9 cm and a rim thickness of 2.1 cm. Its condition is notably poor. Similar mirrors have been found at the {{ill|Niiyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Nara Prefecture, the {{ill|Manai Kofun|ja|真名井古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Osaka Prefecture, and a site in southern Kyoto (now in the collection of the Kurashiki Archaeological Museum). ** {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 2 ***: One mirror is an eight-flower (Hakka) mirror, with a diameter of 10.14 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. ***: Another mirror is a six-flower (Rokka) mirror, with a diameter of 9.8 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. * Iron axes: 2 *: Rectangular iron axes. It is double-edged, with a remaining length of 18.1 cm and a blade width of 1.0 cm. * Fragments of iron swords: 4 fragmentary pieces. * Fragments of a straight sword: with a remaining length of 22.3 cm. * Fragments of {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 9 pieces, representing three types – a double-mouthed jar, an S-shaped jar with a rim base, and a jar with high shoulders. == Historic Site == === National Important designation1 (National designation1) === * Gunma Prefecture Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun burial goods (archaeological materials) – details are as follows. These items are held by the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and were Historic Site1 on June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}){{国cultural property等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳Excavated Artifacts}}. ** Bronze mirrors: *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror with an inscription indicating the first collectively. ** 2 pieces of fragmented iron axes. ** 1 piece of fragmented iron halberd. ** 9 pieces of fragmented {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (located in Taitō, Tokyo) – holds the burial goods from the Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kani-sawa Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun''' (柴崎蟹沢古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Shibazaki Town, {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its type was either a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The mound is no longer extant, and the burial goods have been Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == It is a small kofun constructed on a low terrace on the southern bank of the Kasu River (粕川), a branch of the Ino River, in the southern part of Gunma Prefecture. In the surrounding area, the distribution of Early Kofun period sites and kofun is known, and to the southeast lies the {{ill|Shibazaki Asama-yama Kofun|ja|柴崎浅間山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), grave goods were excavated, and subsequently the mound was leveled. The original type of the tumulus is unclear; {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|qq=draft}} regarded it as a small {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of about 12 meters in diameter, while {{ill|Motomura Gōshō|ja|本村豪章|qq=draft|qq=draft}} considered it a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with a side length of about 22 meters{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. No thatched stones or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were observed on the mound’s exterior{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. According to records, the burial facility is estimated to have been a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}; its main axis was oriented north–south, and traces of vermilion were found inside{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. During the excavation in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), four bronze mirrors (2 {{ill|triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were recovered{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Notably, one of the triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors bears an inscription indicating the first year of the era "□始元年" (presumably the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the Wei, i.e. {{ill|340|ja|340|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), making it an important example of the early stage in the development of such mirrors{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The construction period is estimated to be in the latter half of the 4th century of the Early Kofun period{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Despite its small size, this kofun is significant because, in addition to yielding one of the few dated mirrors in Gunma Prefecture, it also contained four bronze mirrors as grave goods. These features—unreflected in the tumulus’s type or map_size—suggest a connection with the Yamato state in the Kinai region, similar only to the {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|ja|長者屋敷天王山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Gunma Prefecture{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Site History == * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), grave goods were excavated{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * In {{ill|1926|ja|1926|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Showa era|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|qq=draft}} conducted a survey (at which time the mound had been leveled and converted to farmland){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * On June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Heisei 2), a survey was conducted on residential land in the Shimo-Ōruimachi area, but the kofun could not be confirmed (as recorded in the Takasaki City History){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. == burial goods == [[File:蟹沢古墳出土 三角縁同向式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Triangular-edged, same-facing Shikishinju Mirror
(National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}})}}{{small|{{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} exhibition.}}]] The grave goods excavated in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are as follows{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}: * Bronze mirrors: 4 ** {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 2 ***: One mirror is a triangular-edged, same-facing (stair-step type) Shikishinju mirror, with a diameter of 22.6 cm and a rim thickness of 1.0 cm. It bears the inscription "□始元年 陳是作鏡 自有經迷 本自𦭴師 杜地命出 壽如金石 保子□□" and is presumed to date to the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|340|ja|340|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) of the Wei, representing an early stage of triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors. Mirrors of similar type have been found at the {{ill|Moriho Kofun|ja|森尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Hyogo Prefecture and the {{ill|Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|ja|竹島御家老屋敷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Yamaguchi Prefecture. ***: Another mirror is a triangular-edged mirror depicting three gods and three beasts, with a diameter of 21.9 cm and a rim thickness of 2.1 cm. Its condition is notably poor. Similar mirrors have been found at the {{ill|Niiyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Nara Prefecture, the {{ill|Manai Kofun|ja|真名井古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Osaka Prefecture, and a site in southern Kyoto (now in the collection of the Kurashiki Archaeological Museum). ** {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 2 ***: One mirror is an eight-flower (Hakka) mirror, with a diameter of 10.14 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. ***: Another mirror is a six-flower (Rokka) mirror, with a diameter of 9.8 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. * Iron axes: 2 *: Rectangular iron axes. One piece measures 12.4 cm in length, 4.3 cm in width, and 1.0 cm in thickness; the other measures 8.4 cm in length, 4.4 cm in width, and 1.3 cm in thickness. * Iron adze: 1 *: Some sources refer to it as an iron spear. It is double-edged, with a remaining length of 18.1 cm and a blade width of 1.0 cm. * Fragments of iron swords: 4 fragmentary pieces. * Fragments of a straight sword: with a remaining length of 22.3 cm. * Fragments of {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 9 pieces, representing three types – a double-mouthed jar, an S-shaped jar with a rim base, and a jar with high shoulders. == Historic Site == === National Important designation1 (National designation1) === * Gunma Prefecture Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun burial goods (archaeological materials) – details are as follows. These items are held by the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and were Historic Site1 on June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}){{国cultural property等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳Excavated Artifacts}}. ** Bronze mirrors: *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror with an inscription indicating the first year of □始元年. *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror. *** 2 Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors. ** Swords and related items: *** Fragments of iron swords – collectively. *** Fragments of iron sword remnants – collectively. ** 2 pieces of fragmented iron axes. ** 1 piece of fragmented iron halberd. ** 9 pieces of fragmented {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (located in Taitō, Tokyo) – holds the burial goods from the Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|lt=Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|蟹沢古墳（古墳）|1989}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=|year=1999|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title=New Edited Takasaki City History|volume=Materials Edition 1: Early Ancient I|publisher={{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Bronze No thatched stones or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were observed on the mound’s exterior{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. According to records, the burial facility is estimated to have been a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}; its main axis was oriented north–south, and traces of vermilion were found inside{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. During the excavation in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), four bronze mirrors (2 {{ill|triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were recovered{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Notably, one of the triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors bears an inscription indicating the first year of the era "□始元年" (presumably the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the Wei, i.e. {{ill|340|ja|340|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), making it an important example of the early stage in the development of such mirrors{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The construction period is estimated to be in the latter half of the 4th century of the Early Kofun period{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Despite its small size, this kofun is significant because, in addition to yielding one of the few dated mirrors in Gunma Prefecture, it also contained four bronze mirrors as grave goods. These features—unreflected in the tumulus’s type or map_size—suggest a connection with the Yamato state in the Kinai region, similar only to the {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|ja|長者屋敷天王山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Gunma Prefecture{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Site History == * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), grave goods were excavated{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * In {{ill|1926|ja|1926|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Showa era|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|qq=draft}} conducted a survey (at which time the mound had been leveled and converted to farmland){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * On June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Heisei 2), a survey was conducted on residential land in the Shimo-Ōruimachi area, but the kofun could not be confirmed (as recorded in the Takasaki City History){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. == burial goods == [[File:蟹沢古墳出土 三角縁同向式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Triangular-edged, same-facing Shikishinju Mirror
(National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}})}}{{small|{{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} exhibition.}}]] The grave goods excavated in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} are as follows{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}: * Bronze mirrors: 4 ** {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 2 ***: One mirror is a triangular-edged, same-facing (stair-step type) Shikishinju mirror, with a diameter of 22.6 cm and a rim thickness of 1.0 cm. It bears the inscription "□始元年 陳是作鏡 自有經迷 本自𦭴師 杜地命出 壽如金石 保子□□" and is presumed to date to the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|340|ja|340|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) of the Wei, representing an early stage of triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors. Mirrors of similar type have been found at the {{ill|Moriho Kofun|ja|森尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Hyogo Prefecture and the {{ill|Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|ja|竹島御家老屋敷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Yamaguchi Prefecture. ***: Another mirror is a triangular-edged mirror depicting three gods and three beasts, with a diameter of 21.9 cm and a rim thickness of 2.1 cm. Its condition is notably poor. Similar mirrors have been found at the {{ill|Niiyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Nara Prefecture, the {{ill|Manai Kofun|ja|真名井古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Osaka Prefecture, and a site in southern Kyoto (now in the collection of the Kurashiki Archaeological Museum). ** {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 2 ***: One mirror is an eight-flower (Hakka) mirror, with a diameter of 10.14 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. ***: Another mirror is a six-flower (Rokka) mirror, with a diameter of 9.8 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. * Iron axes: 2 *: Rectangular iron axes. One piece measures 12.4 cm in length, 4.3 cm in width, and 1.0 cm in thickness; the other measures 8.4 cm in length, 4.4 cm in width, and 1.3 cm in thickness. * Iron adze: 1 *: Some sources refer to it as an iron spear. It is double-edged, with a remaining length of 18.1 cm and a blade width of 1.0 cm. * Fragments of iron swords: 4 fragmentary pieces. * Fragments of a straight sword: with a remaining length of 22.3 cm. * Fragments of {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: 9 pieces, representing three types – a double-mouthed jar, an S-shaped jar with a rim base, and a jar with high shoulders. == Historic Site == === National Important designation1 (National designation1) === * Gunma Prefecture Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun burial goods (archaeological materials) – details are as follows. These items are held by the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and were Historic Site1 on June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}){{国cultural property等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳Excavated Artifacts}}. ** Bronze mirrors: *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror with an inscription indicating the first year of □始元年. *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror. *** 2 Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors. ** Swords and related items: *** Fragments of iron swords – collectively. *** Fragments of iron sword remnants – collectively. ** 2 pieces of fragmented iron axes. ** 1 piece of fragmented iron halberd. ** 9 pieces of fragmented {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (located in Taitō, Tokyo) – holds the burial goods from the Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Kani-sawa Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun''' (柴崎蟹沢古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Shibazaki Town, {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Its type was either a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The mound is no longer extant, and the burial goods have been Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == It is a small kofun constructed on a low terrace on the southern bank of the Kasu River (粕川), a branch of the Ino River, in the southern part of Gunma Prefecture. In the surrounding area, the distribution of Early Kofun period sites and kofun is known, and to the southeast lies the {{ill|Shibazaki Asama-yama Kofun|ja|柴崎浅間山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), grave goods were excavated, and subsequently the mound was leveled. The original type of the tumulus is unclear; {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|qq=draft}} regarded it as a small {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of about 12 meters in diameter, while {{ill|Motomura Gōshō|ja|本村豪章|qq=draft|qq=draft}} considered it a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} with a side length of about 22 meters{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. No thatched stones or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were observed on the mound’s exterior{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. According to records, the burial facility is estimated to have been a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}; its main axis was oriented north–south, and traces of vermilion were found inside{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. During the excavation in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), four bronze mirrors (2 {{ill|triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} were recovered{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Notably, one of the triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors bears an inscription indicating the first year of the era "□始元年" (presumably the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the Wei, i.e. {{ill|340|ja|340|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), making it an important example of the early stage in the development of such mirrors{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The construction period is estimated to be in the latter half of the 4th century of the Early Kofun period{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Despite its small size, this kofun is significant because, in addition to yielding one of the few dated mirrors in Gunma Prefecture, it also contained four bronze mirrors as grave goods. These features—unreflected in the tumulus’s type or map_size—suggest a connection with the Yamato state in the Kinai region, similar only to the {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|ja|長者屋敷天王山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Gunma Prefecture{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == Site History == * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), grave goods were excavated{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * In {{ill|1926|ja|1926|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Showa era|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|qq=draft}} conducted a survey (at which time the mound had been leveled and converted to farmland){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * On June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Heisei 2), a survey was conducted on residential land in the Shimo-Ōruimachi area, but the kofun could not be confirmed (as recorded in the Takasaki City History){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. == burial goods == [[File:蟹沢古墳出土 三角縁同向式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Triangular-edged, same-facing Shikishinju Mirror

collectively. ** 2 pieces of fragmented iron axes. ** 1 piece of fragmented iron halberd. ** 9 pieces of fragmented {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (located in Taitō, Tokyo) – holds the burial goods from the Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Saburō Kobayashi (archaeologist)|ja|小林三郎 (考古学者)|lt=Saburō Kobayashi|lt_ja=小林三郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|year=1989|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|lt=Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|蟹沢古墳（古墳）|1989}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=|year=1999|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title=New Edited Takasaki City History|volume=Materials Edition 1: Early Ancient I|publisher={{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Bronze
