So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = {{ill|Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|qq=}} |image = |alternate_name = | part_of = |location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki City|qq=}} Shibazaki Town (柴崎町) |coordinates = 36 |coordinates = 18 |coordinates = 52.58 |coordinates = 139 |coordinates = 3 |coordinates = 24.43 |omitted = JP-10 |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} or {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} |map_size = Diameter 12 m or side length 22 m |burial = (Estimated) {{ill|Clay Coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay Coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} |excavations = 2 {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|qq=}}s (including 1 bronze mirror with an inscription from the first year of the Seishi era) and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Flower mirror|qq=}}s, as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogū|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} |Secondary tumulus = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/4th century|qq=}} |Interred = |Mausoleum = |Historic Site = None |Tangible designation1 = excavations (National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important designation1|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}) |notes = The mound is no longer extant |map = Japan Gunma |type = |map_dot_label = Shibazaki
Kani-sawa Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun''' (柴崎蟹沢古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|qq=}} located in Shibazaki Town, {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefecture|qq=}}. Its type was either a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} or a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is no longer extant, and the burial goods have been Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important designation1|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}. == Overview == It is a small kofun constructed on a low terrace on the southern bank of the Kasu River (粕川), a branch of the Ino River, in the southern part of Gunma Prefecture. In the surrounding area, the distribution of Early Kofun period sites and kofun is known, and to the southeast lies the {{ill|Shibazaki Asama-yama Kofun|ja|柴崎浅間山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibazaki Asama-yama Kofun|qq=}}. In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1909|qq=}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}), grave goods were excavated, and subsequently the mound was leveled. The original type of the tumulus is unclear; {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morimoto Rokujirō|qq=}} regarded it as a small {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} of about 12 meters in diameter, while {{ill|Motomura Gōshō|ja|本村豪章|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motomura Gōshō|qq=}} considered it a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} with a side length of about 22 meters{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. No thatched stones or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} were observed on the mound’s exterior{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. According to records, the burial facility is estimated to have been a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}; its main axis was oriented north–south, and traces of vermilion were found inside{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. During the excavation in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1909|qq=}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}), four bronze mirrors (2 {{ill|triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|qq=}} and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Flower mirror|qq=}}) as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogū|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} were recovered{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Notably, one of the triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors bears an inscription indicating the first year of the era "□始元年" (presumably the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishi era (Wei)|qq=}} of the Wei, i.e. {{ill|340|ja|340|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/340|qq=}}), making it an important example of the early stage in the development of such mirrors{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The construction period is estimated to be in the latter half of the 4th century of the Early Kofun period{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Despite its small size, this kofun is significant because, in addition to yielding one of the few dated mirrors in Gunma Prefecture, it also contained four bronze mirrors as grave goods. These features—unreflected in the tumulus’s type or map_size—suggest a connection with the Yamato state in the Kinai region, similar only to the {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|ja|長者屋敷天王山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|qq=}} in Gunma Prefecture{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important designation1|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} in {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1989|qq=}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}}). == Site History == * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1909|qq=}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}), grave goods were excavated{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * In {{ill|1926|ja|1926|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1926|qq=}} (the first year of the {{ill|Showa era|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa era|qq=}}), {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morimoto Rokujirō|qq=}} conducted a survey (at which time the mound had been leveled and converted to farmland){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * On June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1989|qq=}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}}), the burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important designation1|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1990|qq=}} (Heisei 2), a survey was conducted on residential land in the Shimo-Ōruimachi area, but the kofun could not be confirmed (as recorded in the Takasaki City History){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. == burial goods == [[File:蟹沢古墳出土 三角縁同向式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Triangular-edged, same-facing Shikishinju Mirror
(National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important designation1|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}})}}{{small|{{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo National Museum|qq=}} exhibition.}}]] The grave goods excavated in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1909|qq=}} are as follows{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}: * Bronze mirrors: 4 ** {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|qq=}}: 2 ***: One mirror is a triangular-edged, same-facing (stair-step type) Shikishinju mirror, with a diameter of 22.6 cm and a rim thickness of 1.0 cm. It bears the inscription "□始元年 陳是作鏡 自有經迷 本自𦭴師 杜地命出 壽如金石 保子□□" and is presumed to date to the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishi era (Wei)|qq=}} ({{ill|340|ja|340|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/340|qq=}}) of the Wei, representing an early stage of triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors. Mirrors of similar type have been found at the {{ill|Moriho Kofun|ja|森尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriho Kofun|qq=}} in Hyōgo Prefecture and the {{ill|Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|ja|竹島御家老屋敷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|qq=}} in Yamaguchi Prefecture. ***: Another mirror is a triangular-edged mirror depicting three gods and three beasts, with a diameter of 21.9 cm and a rim thickness of 2.1 cm. Its condition is notably poor. Similar mirrors have been found at the {{ill|Niiyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiyama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture, the {{ill|Manai Kofun|ja|真名井古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manai Kofun|qq=}} in Osaka Prefecture, and a site in southern Kyoto (now in the collection of the Kurashiki Archaeological Museum). ** {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Flower mirror|qq=}}: 2 ***: One mirror is an eight-flower (Hakka) mirror, with a diameter of 10.14 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. ***: Another mirror is a six-flower (Rokka) mirror, with a diameter of 9.8 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. * Iron axes: 2 *: Rectangular iron axes. One piece measures 12.4 cm in length, 4.3 cm in width, and 1.0 cm in thickness; the other measures 8.4 cm in length, 4.4 cm in width, and 1.3 cm in thickness. * Iron adze: 1 *: Some sources refer to it as an iron spear. It is double-edged, with a remaining length of 18.1 cm and a blade width of 1.0 cm. * Fragments of iron swords: 4 fragmentary pieces. * Fragments of a straight sword: with a remaining length of 22.3 cm. * Fragments of {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogū|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}}: 9 pieces, representing three types – a double-mouthed jar, an S-shaped jar with a rim base, and a jar with high shoulders. == Historic Site == === National Important designation1 (National designation1) === * Gunma Prefecture Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun burial goods (archaeological materials) – details are as follows. These items are held by the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo National Museum|qq=}} and were Historic Site1 on June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1989|qq=}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}}){{国cultural property等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳Excavated Artifacts}}. ** Bronze mirrors: *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror with an inscription indicating the first year of □始元年. *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror. *** 2 Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors. ** Swords and related items: *** Fragments of iron swords – collectively. *** Fragments of iron sword remnants – collectively. ** 2 pieces of fragmented iron axes. ** 1 piece of fragmented iron halberd. ** 9 pieces of fragmented {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogū|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo National Museum|qq=}} (located in Taitō, Tokyo) – holds the burial goods from the Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Saburō Kobayashi (archaeologist)|ja|小林三郎 (考古学者)|lt=Saburō Kobayashi|lt_ja=小林三郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saburō Kobayashi (archaeologist)|qq=}}|year=1989|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|lt=Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Shuppan|qq=}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|蟹沢古墳（古墳）|1989}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=|year=1999|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title=New Edited Takasaki City History|volume=Materials Edition 1: Early Ancient I|publisher={{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki City|qq=}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Bronze mirror#Inscriptions|ja|銅鏡#紀年銘鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirror#Inscriptions|qq=}} == External Links == * {{国cultural property等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳Excavated Artifacts}} of Tokyo]] }} {{translated page|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳|version=101952536|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:ted]] [[Category:Ancient Tombs in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takasaki City]] [[Category:National Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] }} [[sn:Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun]] [[ja:%E6%9F%B4%E5%B4%8E%E8%9F%B9%E6%B2%A2%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[en:Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun]] [[ja:柴崎蟹沢古墳]]

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = {{ill|Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|qq=}} |image = |alternate_name = | part_of = |location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki City|qq=}} Shibazaki Town (柴崎町) |coordinates = 36 |coordinates = 18 |coordinates = 52.58 |coordinates = 139 |coordinates = 3 |coordinates = 24.43 |omitted = JP-10 |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} or {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} |map_size = Diameter 12 m or side length 22 m |burial = (Estimated) {{ill|Clay Coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay Coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} |excavations = 2 {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|qq=}}s (including 1 bronze mirror with an inscription from the first year of the Seishi era) and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Flower mirror|qq=}}s, as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogū|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} |Secondary tumulus = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/4th century|qq=}} |Interred = |Mausoleum = |Historic Site = None |Tangible designation1 = excavations (National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important designation1|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}) |notes = The mound is no longer extant |map = Japan Gunma |type = |map_dot_label = Shibazaki
Kani-sawa Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun''' (柴崎蟹沢古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|qq=}} located in Shibazaki Town, {{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefecture|qq=}}. Its type was either a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} or a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is no longer extant, and the burial goods have been Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important designation1|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}. == Overview == It is a small kofun constructed on a low terrace on the southern bank of the Kasu River (粕川), a branch of the Ino River, in the southern part of Gunma Prefecture. In the surrounding area, the distribution of Early Kofun period sites and kofun is known, and to the southeast lies the {{ill|Shibazaki Asama-yama Kofun|ja|柴崎浅間山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibazaki Asama-yama Kofun|qq=}}. In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1909|qq=}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}), grave goods were excavated, and subsequently the mound was leveled. The original type of the tumulus is unclear; {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morimoto Rokujirō|qq=}} regarded it as a small {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} of about 12 meters in diameter, while {{ill|Motomura Gōshō|ja|本村豪章|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motomura Gōshō|qq=}} considered it a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} with a side length of about 22 meters{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. No thatched stones or {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}} were observed on the mound’s exterior{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. According to records, the burial facility is estimated to have been a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}; its main axis was oriented north–south, and traces of vermilion were found inside{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. During the excavation in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1909|qq=}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}), four bronze mirrors (2 {{ill|triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|qq=}} and 2 {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors|13=User:Immanuelle/Flower mirror|qq=}}) as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogū|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} were recovered{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Notably, one of the triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors bears an inscription indicating the first year of the era "□始元年" (presumably the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishi era (Wei)|qq=}} of the Wei, i.e. {{ill|340|ja|340|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/340|qq=}}), making it an important example of the early stage in the development of such mirrors{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The construction period is estimated to be in the latter half of the 4th century of the Early Kofun period{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Despite its small size, this kofun is significant because, in addition to yielding one of the few dated mirrors in Gunma Prefecture, it also contained four bronze mirrors as grave goods. These features—unreflected in the tumulus’s type or map_size—suggest a connection with the Yamato state in the Kinai region, similar only to the {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|ja|長者屋敷天王山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|qq=}} in Gunma Prefecture{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important designation1|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} in {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1989|qq=}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}}). == Site History == * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1909|qq=}} (the 42nd year of the {{ill|Meiji era|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}), grave goods were excavated{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * In {{ill|1926|ja|1926|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1926|qq=}} (the first year of the {{ill|Showa era|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa era|qq=}}), {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morimoto Rokujirō|qq=}} conducted a survey (at which time the mound had been leveled and converted to farmland){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * On June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1989|qq=}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}}), the burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important designation1|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1990|qq=}} (Heisei 2), a survey was conducted on residential land in the Shimo-Ōruimachi area, but the kofun could not be confirmed (as recorded in the Takasaki City History){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. == burial goods == [[File:蟹沢古墳出土 三角縁同向式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Triangular-edged, same-facing Shikishinju Mirror
(National {{ill|Important designation1|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important designation1|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}})}}{{small|{{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo National Museum|qq=}} exhibition.}}]] The grave goods excavated in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1909|qq=}} are as follows{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}: * Bronze mirrors: 4 ** {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|qq=}}: 2 ***: One mirror is a triangular-edged, same-facing (stair-step type) Shikishinju mirror, with a diameter of 22.6 cm and a rim thickness of 1.0 cm. It bears the inscription "□始元年 陳是作鏡 自有經迷 本自𦭴師 杜地命出 壽如金石 保子□□" and is presumed to date to the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishi era (Wei)|qq=}} ({{ill|340|ja|340|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/340|qq=}}) of the Wei, representing an early stage of triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors. Mirrors of similar type have been found at the {{ill|Moriho Kofun|ja|森尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriho Kofun|qq=}} in Hyōgo Prefecture and the {{ill|Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|ja|竹島御家老屋敷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|qq=}} in Yamaguchi Prefecture. ***: Another mirror is a triangular-edged mirror depicting three gods and three beasts, with a diameter of 21.9 cm and a rim thickness of 2.1 cm. Its condition is notably poor. Similar mirrors have been found at the {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|lt=Shinyama Kofun|lt=Niiyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiyama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture, the {{ill|Manai Kofun|ja|真名井古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manai Kofun|qq=}} in Osaka Prefecture, and a site in southern Kyoto (now in the collection of the Kurashiki Archaeological Museum). ** {{ill|Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naikō Hanabumi Mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Flower mirror|qq=}}: 2 ***: One mirror is an eight-flower (Hakka) mirror, with a diameter of 10.14 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. ***: Another mirror is a six-flower (Rokka) mirror, with a diameter of 9.8 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. * Iron axes: 2 *: Rectangular iron axes. One piece measures 12.4 cm in length, 4.3 cm in width, and 1.0 cm in thickness; the other measures 8.4 cm in length, 4.4 cm in width, and 1.3 cm in thickness. * Iron adze: 1 *: Some sources refer to it as an iron spear. It is double-edged, with a remaining length of 18.1 cm and a blade width of 1.0 cm. * Fragments of iron swords: 4 fragmentary pieces. * Fragments of a straight sword: with a remaining length of 22.3 cm. * Fragments of {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogū|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}}: 9 pieces, representing three types – a double-mouthed jar, an S-shaped jar with a rim base, and a jar with high shoulders. == Historic Site == === National Important designation1 (National designation1) === * Gunma Prefecture Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun burial goods (archaeological materials) – details are as follows. These items are held by the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo National Museum|qq=}} and were Historic Site1 on June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1989|qq=}} (the first year of the {{ill|Heisei era|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei era|qq=}}){{国cultural property等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳Excavated Artifacts}}. ** Bronze mirrors: *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror with an inscription indicating the first year of □始元年. *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror. *** 2 Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors. ** Swords and related items: *** Fragments of iron swords – collectively. *** Fragments of iron sword remnants – collectively. ** 2 pieces of fragmented iron axes. ** 1 piece of fragmented iron halberd. ** 9 pieces of fragmented {{ill|Dogū|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dogū|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo National Museum|qq=}} (located in Taitō, Tokyo) – holds the burial goods from the Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Saburō Kobayashi (archaeologist)|ja|小林三郎 (考古学者)|lt=Saburō Kobayashi|lt_ja=小林三郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saburō Kobayashi (archaeologist)|qq=}}|year=1989|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|lt=Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Shuppan|qq=}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|蟹沢古墳（古墳）|1989}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=|year=1999|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title=New Edited Takasaki City History|volume=Materials Edition 1: Early Ancient I|publisher={{ill|Takasaki City|ja|高崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasaki City|qq=}}|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Bronze mirror#Inscriptions|ja|銅鏡#紀年銘鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirror#Inscriptions|qq=}} == External Links == * {{国cultural property等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳Excavated Artifacts}} of Tokyo]] }} {{translated page|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳|version=101952536|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:ted]] [[Category:Ancient Tombs in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takasaki City]] [[Category:National Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] }} [[sn:Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun]] Kani-sawa Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun''' (柴崎蟹沢古墳) was a [[:en:Kofun|kofun]] located in Shibazaki Town, [[:en:Takasaki|Takasaki City]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. Its type was either a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} or a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is no longer extant, and the burial goods have been Historic Site1 as a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == Overview == It is a small kofun constructed on a low terrace on the southern bank of the Kasu River (粕川), a branch of the Ino River, in the southern part of Gunma Prefecture. In the surrounding area, the distribution of Early Kofun period sites and kofun is known, and to the southeast lies the {{ill|Shibazaki Asama-yama Kofun|ja|柴崎浅間山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibazaki Asama-yama Kofun|qq=}}. In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1909|qq=}} (the 42nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), grave goods were excavated, and subsequently the mound was leveled. The original type of the tumulus is unclear; {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morimoto Rokujirō|qq=}} regarded it as a small {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} of about 12 meters in diameter, while {{ill|Motomura Gōshō|ja|本村豪章|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motomura Gōshō|qq=}} considered it a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} with a side length of about 22 meters{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. No thatched stones or [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] were observed on the mound’s exterior{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. According to records, the burial facility is estimated to have been a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}; its main axis was oriented north–south, and traces of vermilion were found inside{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. During the excavation in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1909|qq=}} (the 42nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), four bronze mirrors (2 {{ill|triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|qq=}} and 2 [[:en:Flower mirror|Flower mirror]]) as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were recovered{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Notably, one of the triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors bears an inscription indicating the first year of the era "□始元年" (presumably the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishi era (Wei)|qq=}} of the Wei, i.e. {{ill|340|ja|340|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/340|qq=}}), making it an important example of the early stage in the development of such mirrors{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The construction period is estimated to be in the latter half of the 4th century of the Early Kofun period{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Despite its small size, this kofun is significant because, in addition to yielding one of the few dated mirrors in Gunma Prefecture, it also contained four bronze mirrors as grave goods. These features—unreflected in the tumulus’s type or map_size—suggest a connection with the Yamato state in the Kinai region, similar only to the {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|ja|長者屋敷天王山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|qq=}} in Gunma Prefecture{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1989|qq=}} (the first year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]]). == Site History == * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1909|qq=}} (the 42nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), grave goods were excavated{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * In {{ill|1926|ja|1926|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1926|qq=}} (the first year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa era]]), {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morimoto Rokujirō|qq=}} conducted a survey (at which time the mound had been leveled and converted to farmland){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * On June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1989|qq=}} (the first year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]]), the burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1990|qq=}} (Heisei 2), a survey was conducted on residential land in the Shimo-Ōruimachi area, but the kofun could not be confirmed (as recorded in the Takasaki City History){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. == burial goods == [[File:蟹沢古墳出土 三角縁同向式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Triangular-edged, same-facing Shikishinju Mirror
(National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]])}}{{small|[[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] exhibition.}}]] The grave goods excavated in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1909|qq=}} are as follows{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}: * Bronze mirrors: 4 ** {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|qq=}}: 2 ***: One mirror is a triangular-edged, same-facing (stair-step type) Shikishinju mirror, with a diameter of 22.6 cm and a rim thickness of 1.0 cm. It bears the inscription "□始元年 陳是作鏡 自有經迷 本自𦭴師 杜地命出 壽如金石 保子□□" and is presumed to date to the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishi era (Wei)|qq=}} ({{ill|340|ja|340|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/340|qq=}}) of the Wei, representing an early stage of triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors. Mirrors of similar type have been found at the {{ill|Moriho Kofun|ja|森尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriho Kofun|qq=}} in Hyōgo Prefecture and the {{ill|Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|ja|竹島御家老屋敷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|qq=}} in Yamaguchi Prefecture. ***: Another mirror is a triangular-edged mirror depicting three gods and three beasts, with a diameter of 21.9 cm and a rim thickness of 2.1 cm. Its condition is notably poor. Similar mirrors have been found at the {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|lt=Shinyama Kofun|lt=Niiyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiyama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture, the {{ill|Manai Kofun|ja|真名井古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manai Kofun|qq=}} in Osaka Prefecture, and a site in southern Kyoto (now in the collection of the Kurashiki Archaeological Museum). ** [[:en:Flower mirror|Flower mirror]]: 2 ***: One mirror is an eight-flower (Hakka) mirror, with a diameter of 10.14 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. ***: Another mirror is a six-flower (Rokka) mirror, with a diameter of 9.8 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. * Iron axes: 2 *: Rectangular iron axes. One piece measures 12.4 cm in length, 4.3 cm in width, and 1.0 cm in thickness; the other measures 8.4 cm in length, 4.4 cm in width, and 1.3 cm in thickness. * Iron adze: 1 *: Some sources refer to it as an iron spear. It is double-edged, with a remaining length of 18.1 cm and a blade width of 1.0 cm. * Fragments of iron swords: 4 fragmentary pieces. * Fragments of a straight sword: with a remaining length of 22.3 cm. * Fragments of [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]: 9 pieces, representing three types – a double-mouthed jar, an S-shaped jar with a rim base, and a jar with high shoulders. == Historic Site == === National Important designation1 (National designation1) === * Gunma Prefecture Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun burial goods (archaeological materials) – details are as follows. These items are held by the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] and were Historic Site1 on June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1989|qq=}} (the first year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]]){{国cultural property等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳Excavated Artifacts}}. ** Bronze mirrors: *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror with an inscription indicating the first year of □始元年. *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror. *** 2 Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors. ** Swords and related items: *** Fragments of iron swords – collectively. *** Fragments of iron sword remnants – collectively. ** 2 pieces of fragmented iron axes. ** 1 piece of fragmented iron halberd. ** 9 pieces of fragmented [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] (located in Taitō, Tokyo) – holds the burial goods from the Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Saburō Kobayashi (archaeologist)|ja|小林三郎 (考古学者)|lt=Saburō Kobayashi|lt_ja=小林三郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saburō Kobayashi (archaeologist)|qq=}}|year=1989|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|lt=Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Shuppan|qq=}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|蟹沢古墳（古墳）|1989}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=|year=1999|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title=New Edited Takasaki City History|volume=Materials Edition 1: Early Ancient I|publisher=[[:en:Takasaki|Takasaki City]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Bronze mirror#Inscriptions|ja|銅鏡#紀年銘鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirror#Inscriptions|qq=}} == External Links == * {{国cultural property等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳Excavated Artifacts}} of Tokyo]] }} {{translated page|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳|version=101952536|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:ted]] [[Category:Ancient Tombs in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takasaki City]] [[Category:National Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] }} [[sn:Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun]] [[ja:%E6%9F%B4%E5%B4%8E%E8%9F%B9%E6%B2%A2%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[en:Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun]] [[ja:柴崎蟹沢古墳]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = {{ill|Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|qq=}} |image = |alternate_name = | part_of = |location = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]][[:en:Takasaki|Takasaki City]] Shibazaki Town (柴崎町) |coordinates = 36 |coordinates = 18 |coordinates = 52.58 |coordinates = 139 |coordinates = 3 |coordinates = 24.43 |omitted = JP-10 |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} or {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} |map_size = Diameter 12 m or side length 22 m |burial = (Estimated) {{ill|Clay Coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay Coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} |excavations = 2 {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|qq=}}s (including 1 bronze mirror with an inscription from the first year of the Seishi era) and 2 [[:en:Flower mirror|Flower mirror]]s, as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |Secondary tumulus = |built = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] |Interred = |Mausoleum = |Historic Site = None |Tangible designation1 = excavations (National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) |notes = The mound is no longer extant |map = Japan Gunma |type = |map_dot_label = Shibazaki
Kani-sawa Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun''' (柴崎蟹沢古墳) was a [[:en:Kofun|kofun]] located in Shibazaki Town, [[:en:Takasaki|Takasaki City]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. Its type was either a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} or a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is no longer extant, and the burial goods have been Historic Site1 as a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == Overview == It is a small kofun constructed on a low terrace on the southern bank of the Kasu River (粕川), a branch of the Ino River, in the southern part of Gunma Prefecture. In the surrounding area, the distribution of Early Kofun period sites and kofun is known, and to the southeast lies the {{ill|Shibazaki Asama-yama Kofun|ja|柴崎浅間山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibazaki Asama-yama Kofun|qq=}}. In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1909|qq=}} (the 42nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), grave goods were excavated, and subsequently the mound was leveled. The original type of the tumulus is unclear; {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morimoto Rokujirō|qq=}} regarded it as a small {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} of about 12 meters in diameter, while {{ill|Motomura Gōshō|ja|本村豪章|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motomura Gōshō|qq=}} considered it a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} with a side length of about 22 meters{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. No thatched stones or [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] were observed on the mound’s exterior{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. According to records, the burial facility is estimated to have been a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}; its main axis was oriented north–south, and traces of vermilion were found inside{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. During the excavation in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1909|qq=}} (the 42nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), four bronze mirrors (2 {{ill|triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|qq=}} and 2 [[:en:Flower mirror|Flower mirror]]) as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were recovered{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Notably, one of the triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors bears an inscription indicating the first year of the era "□始元年" (presumably the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishi era (Wei)|qq=}} of the Wei, i.e. {{ill|340|ja|340|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/340|qq=}}), making it an important example of the early stage in the development of such mirrors{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The construction period is estimated to be in the latter half of the 4th century of the Early Kofun period{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Despite its small size, this kofun is significant because, in addition to yielding one of the few dated mirrors in Gunma Prefecture, it also contained four bronze mirrors as grave goods. These features—unreflected in the tumulus’s type or map_size—suggest a connection with the Yamato state in the Kinai region, similar only to the {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|ja|長者屋敷天王山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|qq=}} in Gunma Prefecture{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1989|qq=}} (the first year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]]). == Site History == * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1909|qq=}} (the 42nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), grave goods were excavated{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * In {{ill|1926|ja|1926|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1926|qq=}} (the first year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa era]]), {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morimoto Rokujirō|qq=}} conducted a survey (at which time the mound had been leveled and converted to farmland){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * On June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1989|qq=}} (the first year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]]), the burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1990|qq=}} (Heisei 2), a survey was conducted on residential land in the Shimo-Ōruimachi area, but the kofun could not be confirmed (as recorded in the Takasaki City History){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. == burial goods == [[File:蟹沢古墳出土 三角縁同向式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Triangular-edged, same-facing Shikishinju Mirror
(National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]])}}{{small|[[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] exhibition.}}]] The grave goods excavated in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1909|qq=}} are as follows{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}: * Bronze mirrors: 4 ** {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|qq=}}: 2 ***: One mirror is a triangular-edged, same-facing (stair-step type) Shikishinju mirror, with a diameter of 22.6 cm and a rim thickness of 1.0 cm. It bears the inscription "□始元年 陳是作鏡 自有經迷 本自𦭴師 杜地命出 壽如金石 保子□□" and is presumed to date to the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishi era (Wei)|qq=}} ({{ill|340|ja|340|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/340|qq=}}) of the Wei, representing an early stage of triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors. Mirrors of similar type have been found at the {{ill|Moriho Kofun|ja|森尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriho Kofun|qq=}} in Hyōgo Prefecture and the {{ill|Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|ja|竹島御家老屋敷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|qq=}} in Yamaguchi Prefecture. ***: Another mirror is a triangular-edged mirror depicting three gods and three beasts, with a diameter of 21.9 cm and a rim thickness of 2.1 cm. Its condition is notably poor. Similar mirrors have been found at the {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|lt=Shinyama Kofun|lt=Niiyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiyama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture, the {{ill|Manai Kofun|ja|真名井古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manai Kofun|qq=}} in Osaka Prefecture, and a site in southern Kyoto (now in the collection of the Kurashiki Archaeological Museum). ** [[:en:Flower mirror|Flower mirror]]: 2 ***: One mirror is an eight-flower (Hakka) mirror, with a diameter of 10.14 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. ***: Another mirror is a six-flower (Rokka) mirror, with a diameter of 9.8 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. * Iron axes: 2 *: Rectangular iron axes. One piece measures 12.4 cm in length, 4.3 cm in width, and 1.0 cm in thickness; the other measures 8.4 cm in length, 4.4 cm in width, and 1.3 cm in thickness. * Iron adze: 1 *: Some sources refer to it as an iron spear. It is double-edged, with a remaining length of 18.1 cm and a blade width of 1.0 cm. * Fragments of iron swords: 4 fragmentary pieces. * Fragments of a straight sword: with a remaining length of 22.3 cm. * Fragments of [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]: 9 pieces, representing three types – a double-mouthed jar, an S-shaped jar with a rim base, and a jar with high shoulders. == Historic Site == === National Important designation1 (National designation1) === * Gunma Prefecture Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun burial goods (archaeological materials) – details are as follows. These items are held by the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] and were Historic Site1 on June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1989|qq=}} (the first year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]]){{国cultural property等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳Excavated Artifacts}}. ** Bronze mirrors: *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror with an inscription indicating the first year of □始元年. *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror. *** 2 Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors. ** Swords and related items: *** Fragments of iron swords – collectively. *** Fragments of iron sword remnants – collectively. ** 2 pieces of fragmented iron axes. ** 1 piece of fragmented iron halberd. ** 9 pieces of fragmented [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] (located in Taitō, Tokyo) – holds the burial goods from the Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Saburō Kobayashi (archaeologist)|ja|小林三郎 (考古学者)|lt=Saburō Kobayashi|lt_ja=小林三郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saburō Kobayashi (archaeologist)|qq=}}|year=1989|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|lt=Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyo-do Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo-do Shuppan|qq=}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|蟹沢古墳（古墳）|1989}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=|year=1999|chapter=Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|title=New Edited Takasaki City History|volume=Materials Edition 1: Early Ancient I|publisher=[[:en:Takasaki|Takasaki City]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Bronze mirror#Inscriptions|ja|銅鏡#紀年銘鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirror#Inscriptions|qq=}} == External Links == * {{国cultural property等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳Excavated Artifacts}} of Tokyo]] }} {{translated page|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳|version=101952536|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:ted]] [[Category:Ancient Tombs in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takasaki City]] [[Category:National Important Cultural Property (Japan)]] }} [[sn:Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun]] [[ja:%E6%9F%B4%E5%B4%8E%E8%9F%B9%E6%B2%A2%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3]] [[en:Shibasaki Kanizawa Kofun]] [[ja:柴崎蟹沢古墳]] [[Category:東京都の重要文化財]]

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Japanese Kofun |name = {{ill|Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|ja|柴崎蟹沢古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun|qq=}} |image = |alternate_name = | part_of = |location = [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]][[:en:Takasaki|Takasaki City]] Shibazaki Town (柴崎町) |coordinates = 36 |coordinates = 18 |coordinates = 52.58 |coordinates = 139 |coordinates = 3 |coordinates = 24.43 |omitted = JP-10 |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} or {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} |map_size = Diameter 12 m or side length 22 m |burial = (Estimated) {{ill|Clay Coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay Coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}} |excavations = 2 {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|qq=}}s (including 1 bronze mirror with an inscription from the first year of the Seishi era) and 2 [[:en:Flower mirror|Flower mirror]]s, as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |Secondary tumulus = |built = Late [[:en:4th century|4th century]] |Interred = |Mausoleum = |Historic Site = None |Tangible designation1 = excavations (National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) |notes = The mound is no longer extant |map = Japan Gunma |type = |map_dot_label = Shibazaki
Kani-sawa Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun''' (柴崎蟹沢古墳) was a [[:en:Kofun|kofun]] located in Shibazaki Town, [[:en:Takasaki|Takasaki City]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. Its type was either a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} or a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}}. The mound is no longer extant, and the burial goods have been Historic Site1 as a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == Overview == It is a small kofun constructed on a low terrace on the southern bank of the Kasu River (粕川), a branch of the Ino River, in the southern part of Gunma Prefecture. In the surrounding area, the distribution of Early Kofun period sites and kofun is known, and to the southeast lies the {{ill|Shibazaki Asama-yama Kofun|ja|柴崎浅間山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibazaki Asama-yama Kofun|qq=}}. In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1909|qq=}} (the 42nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), grave goods were excavated, and subsequently the mound was leveled. The original type of the tumulus is unclear; {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morimoto Rokujirō|qq=}} regarded it as a small {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} of about 12 meters in diameter, while {{ill|Motomura Gōshō|ja|本村豪章|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motomura Gōshō|qq=}} considered it a {{ill|square kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} with a side length of about 22 meters{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. No thatched stones or [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] were observed on the mound’s exterior{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. According to records, the burial facility is estimated to have been a {{ill|clay coffin|ja|粘土槨|1=Clay coffin chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/clay coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Clay coffin chamber|qq=}}{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}; its main axis was oriented north–south, and traces of vermilion were found inside{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. During the excavation in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1909|qq=}} (the 42nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), four bronze mirrors (2 {{ill|triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors|qq=}} and 2 [[:en:Flower mirror|Flower mirror]]) as well as iron axes, iron adzes, iron swords, a straight sword, and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] were recovered{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Notably, one of the triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors bears an inscription indicating the first year of the era "□始元年" (presumably the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishi era (Wei)|qq=}} of the Wei, i.e. {{ill|340|ja|340|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/340|qq=}}), making it an important example of the early stage in the development of such mirrors{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The construction period is estimated to be in the latter half of the 4th century of the Early Kofun period{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. Despite its small size, this kofun is significant because, in addition to yielding one of the few dated mirrors in Gunma Prefecture, it also contained four bronze mirrors as grave goods. These features—unreflected in the tumulus’s type or map_size—suggest a connection with the Yamato state in the Kinai region, similar only to the {{ill|Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|前橋天神山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maebashi Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|ja|長者屋敷天王山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōjayashiki Tennōyama Kofun|qq=}} in Gunma Prefecture{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. The burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1989|qq=}} (the first year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]]). == Site History == * In {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1909|qq=}} (the 42nd year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), grave goods were excavated{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * In {{ill|1926|ja|1926|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1926|qq=}} (the first year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Showa era]]), {{ill|Morimoto Rokujirō|ja|森本六爾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morimoto Rokujirō|qq=}} conducted a survey (at which time the mound had been leveled and converted to farmland){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. * On June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1989|qq=}} (the first year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]]), the burial goods were Historic Site1 as a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. * In {{ill|1990|ja|1990|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1990|qq=}} (Heisei 2), a survey was conducted on residential land in the Shimo-Ōruimachi area, but the kofun could not be confirmed (as recorded in the Takasaki City History){{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}. == burial goods == [[File:蟹沢古墳出土 三角縁同向式神獣鏡.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Triangular-edged, same-facing Shikishinju Mirror
(National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]])}}{{small|[[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] exhibition.}}]] The grave goods excavated in {{ill|1909|ja|1909|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1909|qq=}} are as follows{{Sfn|新編高崎市史 資料編1 原始古代I|1999}}: * Bronze mirrors: 4 ** {{ill|Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror|qq=}}: 2 ***: One mirror is a triangular-edged, same-facing (stair-step type) Shikishinju mirror, with a diameter of 22.6 cm and a rim thickness of 1.0 cm. It bears the inscription "□始元年 陳是作鏡 自有經迷 本自𦭴師 杜地命出 壽如金石 保子□□" and is presumed to date to the first year of the {{ill|Seishi era (Wei)|ja|正始 (魏)|lt=Seishi|lt_ja=正始|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seishi era (Wei)|qq=}} ({{ill|340|ja|340|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/340|qq=}}) of the Wei, representing an early stage of triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirrors. Mirrors of similar type have been found at the {{ill|Moriho Kofun|ja|森尾古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriho Kofun|qq=}} in Hyōgo Prefecture and the {{ill|Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|ja|竹島御家老屋敷古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenoshima Ogieroshi Kofun|qq=}} in Yamaguchi Prefecture. ***: Another mirror is a triangular-edged mirror depicting three gods and three beasts, with a diameter of 21.9 cm and a rim thickness of 2.1 cm. Its condition is notably poor. Similar mirrors have been found at the {{ill|Shinyama Kofun|lt=Shinyama Kofun|lt=Niiyama Kofun|ja|新山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiyama Kofun|qq=}} in Nara Prefecture, the {{ill|Manai Kofun|ja|真名井古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manai Kofun|qq=}} in Osaka Prefecture, and a site in southern Kyoto (now in the collection of the Kurashiki Archaeological Museum). ** [[:en:Flower mirror|Flower mirror]]: 2 ***: One mirror is an eight-flower (Hakka) mirror, with a diameter of 10.14 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. ***: Another mirror is a six-flower (Rokka) mirror, with a diameter of 9.8 cm and a rim thickness of 0.3 cm. * Iron axes: 2 *: Rectangular iron axes. One piece measures 12.4 cm in length, 4.3 cm in width, and 1.0 cm in thickness; the other measures 8.4 cm in length, 4.4 cm in width, and 1.3 cm in thickness. * Iron adze: 1 *: Some sources refer to it as an iron spear. It is double-edged, with a remaining length of 18.1 cm and a blade width of 1.0 cm. * Fragments of iron swords: 4 fragmentary pieces. * Fragments of a straight sword: with a remaining length of 22.3 cm. * Fragments of [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]: 9 pieces, representing three types – a double-mouthed jar, an S-shaped jar with a rim base, and a jar with high shoulders. == Historic Site == === National Important designation1 (National designation1) === * Gunma Prefecture Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun burial goods (archaeological materials) – details are as follows. These items are held by the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] and were Historic Site1 on June 12, {{ill|1989|ja|1989|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1989|qq=}} (the first year of the [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei era]]){{国cultural property等データベース|201|10061|群馬県蟹沢古墳Excavated Artifacts}}. ** Bronze mirrors: *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror with an inscription indicating the first year of □始元年. *** 1 triangular-edged Shikishinju Mirror. *** 2 Naikō Hanabumi Mirrors. ** Swords and related items: *** Fragments of iron swords – collectively. *** Fragments of iron sword remnants – collectively. ** 2 pieces of fragmented iron axes. ** 1 piece of fragmented iron halberd. ** 9 pieces of fragmented [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]]. == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] (located in Taitō, Tokyo) – holds the burial goods from the Shibazaki Kani-sawa Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author={{ill|Saburō Kobayashi (archaeologist)|ja|小林三郎 (考古学者)|lt=Saburō Kobayashi|lt_ja=小林三郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saburō Kobayashi
